questions.text: Which of the following substances exhibits hydrogen bonding?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding occurs in those substances in which a highly electronegative atom (such as Fluorine (F), Oxygen (O) or Nitrogen (N)) is directly bonded to Hydrogen. The presence of one of these three atoms is a REQUIREMENT for hydrogen bonding to occur. Only option C mentions a substance which has one of these three atoms, i.e. Nitrogen in NH3, while the other options mention other atoms aside from the three highly electronegative species mentioned previously.

questions.text: The substance which conducts electricity by the movement of ions:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carbon atom has 4 electrons in its outer shell. This delocalised electron can move move freely between the carbon layers of graphite and conduct electricity. However, in the solid state, ionic compounds such as sodium chloride have their ions fixed in position and therefore these ions cannot move so solid ionic compounds cannot conduct electricity. However in the molten state, ions in ionic compounds are free to flow and therefore molten sodium chloride can conduct electricity.

questions.text: The shape of CO2 molecule is similar to:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: CO2 has a linear structure with a bond angle of 180 degrees with the central atom (C) having no lone pairs and being sp hybrid. Beryllium in BeF2 has the same configuration. Other options are wrong because: H2S= sp3 SO2=sp2 SnCl2=sp2

questions.text: Both ionic and covalent bonds are present in:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: NaOH is formed by Na+ (cation) and anion (OH-). Thus, It contains ionic bonds. On the other hand, if we consider only the hydroxyl group (-OH), then we will see that a covalent bond is present.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has the largest number of shared pairs of electrons?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: C2H4 has the largest number of shared electrons as it has 12 valence electrons in total which are all part of a shared pair (4 electrons between the two central carbons and 2 electrons each between 4 carbon-Hydrogen bonds). N2 has 3 shared pairs of electron (6 electrons in total) CO2 has 4 shared pairs of electrons (8 electrons in total). NH3 has 3 shared pairs of electrons. (6 electrons in total)

questions.text: The decreasing order of second ionization energy of K, Ca, Ba is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: As we move down the group size increases and the electrons experience less effective nuclear charge. Due to this reason, ionization potential decreases down the group. Thus, the second ionization potential of Ca is greater than that of Ba. The second ionization potential of K is greater than that of Ca as K acquires stable noble gas configuration after losing one electron.

questions.text: The common features, among the species CN, CO and NO are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Bond order three and weak field ligands
Bond order two and π acceptors

questions.explanation: This question can be solved by ruling out the wrong options. First of all, CN, CO and NO do not have the same number of electrons so that can be termed as isoelectronic. This rules out options A and D. Option C is also wrong because the bond order of these elements is not two, it is three, and neither they are π acceptors, rather are ligands that possess the ability to donate an electron pair. Hence Option B is the correct option.

questions.text: The correct increasing order of electron affinity value of atoms is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The first Electron affinity depends on how strong the bond is formed between the added electron and the nucleus. We have to take into account the effective nuclear charge and the distance between the electron and the nucleus. Since all the elements given have the same effective nuclear charge, we then consider the distance; the greater the distance the smaller the electron affinity. Based on that, we have the order as F>Cl>Br>I.Electron affinity usually decreases as you move down a group on the periodic table because the atoms in that group get larger. This means that when an electron is added to an atom lower in the group, it's further away from the nucleus than it would be in a smaller atom above it. As a result, the electron is less attracted to the nucleus, which leads to a lower electron affinity.ChlorineHas a larger size than fluorine, allowing it to better accommodate electrons.FluorineThe small size of the fluorine atom causes significant repulsion of incoming electrons.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules has sp3 hybridization?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Option A, CH4 has sp3 hybridization which enables all four bonds to be of the same length and strength. Option B, C2H4 has sp2 hybridization as carbon atom contains three single bonds which include sp2 orbitals, whereas the C-C double bond is brought about by a p-p overlap. Options, C and D, both have sp hybridization. In CO2, carbon has two single bonds which involve sp orbitals, and a double bond with each oxygen so, two free p orbitals are used for the two double bonds. In C2H2, an alkyne, carbon has two single bonds which involve sp orbitals, but a triple bond that uses two p orbitals per carbon atom.

questions.text: An ionic compound is most likely to be formed when:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Ionization energy of A is high but electron affinity of B is low
The ionization energy of A is low but electron affinity of B is high
Both ionization energy of A and electron affinity of B are high
Both ionization energy of A and electron affinity of B are low

questions.explanation: An atom with high electron affinity means it will readily accept an electron from another atom. Hence, we can conclude that an ionic bond is most likely to be formed when the ionization energy of A is low and the electron affinity of B is high.

questions.text: The relation between first and second ionization potentials of a given atom is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Compared to the first ionization potential, the value of second ionization potential of an element is greater. For second ionization potential, an electron is to be removed from uni positive cation. There is the strong electrophilic attractive force between the unipositive ion and the electron to be removed.

questions.explanation: The magnesium and sodium atoms have roughly the same size. What differs is the effective nuclear charge, which is +1 for Na and +2 for Mg. Hence, Mg has a stronger bond between its valence electrons and its nucleus and requires more energy to remove the electron.Compared to the first ionization potential, the value of second ionization potential of an element is greater. For second ionization potential, an electron is to be removed from uni positive cation. There is the strong electrophilic attractive force between the unipositive ion and the electron to be removed.

questions.text: Which of the following has maximum Hydration power?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hydration energy is the amount of energy released when one mole of ions undergoes hydration.Be2+ ion has small size and high charge. Hence, it has high polarizing power and can attract several water molecules. Thus it has the highest hydration energy among the given ions.

questions.text: Linear combination of atomic orbitals (LCAO) results in the formation of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as sigma bond is formed by head-on overlapping of atomic orbitals.
Option B is incorrect as pi bond is formed by parallel or sidewise overlapping of atomic orbitals.
Option C is incorrect as only bonding molecular orbital is not formed rather both bonding and antibonding Molecular Orbitals are formed.

According to Molecular Orbital Theory (MOT), two atomic orbitals overlap resulting in the formation of molecular orbitals. Number of atomic orbitals overlapping together is equal to the molecular orbital formed. The two atomic orbital thus formed by LCAO (linear combination of atomic orbital) in the same phase or in the different phase are known as Bonding Molecular Orbital (BMO) and Antibonding Molecular Orbitals (ABMO) respectively.
Option E is incorrect as the answer cannot be all of these because only option D is correct.

questions.explanation: According to Molecular Orbital Theory (MOT), two atomic orbitals overlap resulting in the formation of molecular orbitals. Number of atomic orbitals overlapping together is equal to the molecular orbital formed. The two atomic orbital thus formed by LCAO (linear combination of atomic orbital) in the same phase or in the different phase are known as Bonding Molecular Orbital (BMO) and Antibonding Molecular Orbitals (ABMO) respectively.

questions.text: The equation shows the reaction between element X and dilute hydrochloric acid: X(s) + 2HCl(aq) -> XCl2(aq) + H2(g) What types of bonding is present in element X and in compound XCl2?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is incorrect as the compound X has metallic bonds, whereas the compound XCl2 has ionic bonds.
It is incorrect as the compound X has metallic bonds, whereas the compound XCl2 has ionic bonds.
It is incorrect as the compound X has metallic bonds, where as the compound XCl2 has ionic bonds.

Metallic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that arises from the electrostatic attractive force between conduction electrons (in the form of an electron cloud of delocalized electrons) and positively charged metal ions. The element X is metal as Metals react with halogens to form salts.The compound XCl2 is a salt and has ionic bonds. As two chlorine atoms are attached, this shows the central metal has two valence electrons in free states, so it is a metal of group II A[Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba].

questions.text: The common features, among the species CN-, CO, and NO+ are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Each of these have same number of electrons;Number of electrons in CO = 6+8 = 14Number of electrons in CN- = 6+7+1 = 14Number of electrons in NO+ = 7+8-1 = 14 The species having same number of electrons are called iso-electronic. Iso-electronic species have same bond order.Bond order is number of chemical bonds present between a pair of atoms. There are 3 chemical bonds between C and O in CO, C and N in CN- and N and O in NO+ so all of these have same bond order of 3.Hence, all the given species are iso- electronic and have same bond order. So, option A is correct.

options.text: Bond order two and π acceptors
options.explanation: CO and CN- are strong field ligands, while NO+ is weak field ligand. Weak field ligands are ones that produce small splittings between the d orbitals and form high spin complexes. Strong field ligands are all π - acceptors. While all have bond order of three.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules have zero Dipole Moment?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CCl4 molecule has zero dipole moment although the C-Cl bonds are polar. This is because the shape of the molecule is tetrahedral and this makes it very symmetrical and the four polar bonds cancel out each other so they have zero Dipole moment.

CO2 has the structure as O=C=O in which there is a bond Dipole moment (C-O bond). But, as the dipole moment of one bond is canceled by another, the structure of CO2 is linear. So, CO2 has zero Dipole moment because it is a linear molecule.
In the case of Cl2, the 2 atoms are identical, so no polarization of the bond is possible, and the Dipole moment is zero.

Benzene has zero Dipole moment. The benzene molecule has a symmetrical planar structure. The structure of benzene is as follows: Carbon and hydrogen in benzene molecules have distinct electronegativities. It has six C - H bonds whose Dipole moments cancel out one another, resulting in zero net Dipole moment.

Dipole moments occur when there is a separation of charge. They can occur between two ions in an ionic bond or between atoms in a covalent bond; dipole moments arise from differences in electronegativity. The larger the difference in electronegativity, the larger the dipole moment. The dipole moment is a measure of the polarity of the molecule. -CCl4 molecule has zero dipole moment although the C-Cl bonds are polar. It has six C - H bonds whose Dipole moments cancel out one another, resulting in zero net Dipole moment.

questions.text: Bond energy: I. is the energy required to break a bond between two atoms in a diatomic molecule II. Is taken as the energy released in forming a bond form free atoms III. Is the measure of the strength of bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: II and III also describe bond energy .
III also describe bond energy .
II also describe bond energy .
I and III also describe bond energy .

When a bond is formed between two atoms, energy is released. The same amount of energy is absorbed when the bond is broken to form neutral atoms. So, the bond energy is the average amount of energy required to break all bonds of a particular type in one mole of the substance. It is determined experimentally, by measuring the heat involved in a chemical reaction. It is also called bond enthalpy, as it is a measure of enthalpy change at 298 K.

questions.explanation: When a bond is formed between two atoms, energy is released. The same amount of energy is absorbed when the bond is broken to form neutral atoms. So, the bond energy is the average amount of energy required to break all bonds of a particular type in one mole of the substance. It is determined experimentally, by measuring the heat involved in a chemical reaction. It is also called bond enthalpy, as it is a measure of enthalpy change at 298 K.

questions.text: sp3 hybridization in CH4 gives it _ geometry.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Linear geometry is shown by sp hybridised molecules.A linear molecule is one in which the atoms are arranged in a straight line (less than a 180° angle). The sp hybridization occurs at the central atom of molecules with linear electron-pair geometries. Carbon dioxide (O=C=O)
Coplanar geometry is shown by sp2 hybridised molecules.in this type one 2s and two 2p orbitals are mixed together to give three equaling and Co planar orbitals

Tetrahedral geometry is shown by sp3 hybridised orbitals like methane.in the formation of methanethe four hybrid atomic orbitals of carbon overlap seperately with four 1s atomic orbitals of hydrogen to form 4 equivalent C-H bonds.

Octahedral molecular geometry, also called square bipyramidal, describes the shape of compounds with six atoms or groups of atoms or ligands symmetrically arranged around a central atom, defining the vertices of an octahedron.
Trigonal pyramid is a molecular geometry with one atom at the apex and three atoms at the corners of a trigonal base, resembling a tetrahedron. Bond angle(s): 90°<θ<109.5°Example: NH3

questions.explanation: Tetrahedral geometry is shown by sp3 hybridised orbitals like methane.in the formation of methanethe four hybrid atomic orbitals of carbon overlap seperately with four 1s atomic orbitals of hydrogen to form 4 equivalent C-H bonds.

questions.text: The boiling point of water is higher than other hydrides because water molecules can form:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Intermolecular hydrogen bonding is responsible for the high boiling point of water (100 °C) compared to the other group 16 hydrides that have no hydrogen bonds. For example if we take comparison of fluorine and H20,Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen, so technically, HF should engage in more hydrogen bonding than water. But the situation is different. Each HF molecule possesses three lone pairs of fluorine and one hydrogen. HF molecules on average make two hydrogen bonds. Each H2O molecule possesses two lone pairs of oxygen. H2O molecules are thus able to form an average of four hydrogen bonds. Hence, water molecules take part in more hydrogen bonding than Hydrofluoric acid. Water molecules form a bulky molecule and it is very difficult to break its bonds. To break all its bonds, a large amount of the energy is required. Thus, H2O has a higher boiling point than HF. Thus h20 can form four hydrogen bonds.
Since 4 hydrogen bonds is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.
Since 4 hydrogen bonds is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.
Since 4 hydrogen bonds is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.

questions.text: GN Lewis described which one of the following bonds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An Ionic bond can be formed after two or more atoms lose or gain electrons to form an ion.

questions.text: The dipole moments of the given molecules (BF3, NF3, NH3) are such that:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refer to main text for explanation.
Refer to main text for explanation.

The dipole moment of a molecule depends on the polarity and the arrangement of the bonds and atoms. In the given molecules, NH3 has the highest dipole moment because it has a polar covalent bond and a lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom, which creates a significant dipole moment. NF3 has a lower dipole moment than NH3 because it has a similar polar covalent bond but has a symmetric shape that cancels out some of the dipole moment. BF3 has the lowest dipole moment of the three molecules because it has only polar covalent bonds and a Trigonal planar shape that cancels out the dipole moments of each bond. Therefore, the correct order of dipole moment is: NH3 > NF3 > BF3 vv

questions.text: Which pair has species with different shapes?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In BeCl2 (beryllium chloride), the central atom (Be) is bonded to two chlorine atoms, forming a linear shape. The molecular geometry is linear. In CO2 (carbon dioxide), the central atom (C) is bonded to two oxygen atoms, forming a linear shape. The molecular geometry is also linear.

In NH3 (ammonia), the central atom (N) is bonded to three hydrogen atoms, forming a pyramidal shape. The molecular geometry is trigonal pyramidal. In BF3 (boron trifluoride), the central atom (B) is bonded to three fluorine atoms, forming a planar triangular shape. The molecular geometry is trigonal planar. Since ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) have different shapes (trigonal pyramidal and trigonal planar, respectively), this pair has species with different shapes.

In SCl2 (sulfur dichloride), the central atom (S) is bonded to two chlorine atoms, forming a bent or angular shape. The molecular geometry is angular. In H2O (water), the central atom (O) is bonded to two hydrogen atoms, forming a bent or angular shape. The molecular geometry is also angular.

In CH4 (methane), the central atom (C) is bonded to four hydrogen atoms, forming a tetrahedral shape. The molecular geometry is tetrahedral. In NH4+ (ammonium ion), the central atom (N) is bonded to four hydrogen atoms, forming a tetrahedral shape. The molecular geometry is also tetrahedral.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is b) NH3 and BF3. These two species have different shapes (trigonal pyramidal and trigonal planar, respectively). The other options have species with the same shapes or molecular geometries.

questions.text: The decreasing order of second ionization energy of K, Ca, Ba is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The second ionization energy is the energy required to remove the second electron from a cation. It is generally greater than the first ionization energy because removing the second electron requires overcoming the attraction between the remaining electrons and the positively charged ion.In the given options, K has the smallest atomic radius among K, Ca, and Ba. As the atomic radius decreases, the attraction between the nucleus and the outermost electron increases, making it harder to remove the second electron. Therefore, K has the highest second ionization energy among the given elements.On the other hand, Ba has the largest atomic radius among the given elements. As the atomic radius increases, the attraction between the nucleus and the outermost electron decreases, making it easier to remove the second electron. Therefore, Ba has the lowest second ionization energy among the given elements.Therefore, the decreasing order of second ionization energy is: K > Ba > Ca.

questions.text: The bonds present in N2O5 are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: N2O5 contains both covalent and ionic bonds. The molecule has a covalent bond between the two nitrogen atoms (N-N bond), which is a sigma bond. Each nitrogen atom also forms a covalent bond with three oxygen atoms (N-O bonds), which are also sigma bonds. However, the fourth oxygen atom is attached to both nitrogen atoms through a coordinate covalent bond, where the lone pair on the oxygen atom is donated to an empty orbital on one of the nitrogen atoms. In addition, the molecule has ionic character due to the presence of an NO2+ ion and an NO3- ion. The NO2+ ion is formed when one of the nitrogen atoms donates a lone pair to an oxygen atom, resulting in a positive charge on the nitrogen atom. The NO3- ion is formed when one of the nitrogen atoms accepts a lone pair from an oxygen atom, resulting in a negative charge on the nitrogen atom.

questions.explanation: In N2O5, the combining atoms are nitrogen and oxygen. As both of them are non-metals. Hence, they share electrons when both of them chemically combine. There are both covalent and coordinate covalent bonds.

questions.text: The shape of SnCl2 is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refer to main text for explanation.
Refer to main text for explanation.

SnCl2 (Tin dichloride) has a bent or V-shaped geometry, which is an example of an angular or non-linear shape. The molecule has a central tin atom that is bonded to two chlorine atoms. The two bond pairs of electrons repel each other, resulting in a bent shape. The bond angle is approximately 119 degrees. This bent shape is due to the lone pair of electrons present on the tin atom. The lone pair occupies a greater amount of space compared to the bond pair, resulting in the distortion of the molecule. In contrast, the linear shape is a molecular geometry in which the atoms of the molecule are arranged in a straight line, like in HCl or CO2. Tetrahedral shape is a molecular geometry that has four atoms attached to a central atom, like in CH4 or CCl4. Trigonal shape is a molecular geometry that has three atoms attached to a central atom, like in BF3.
Refer to main text for explanation.

questions.explanation: SnCl2 (Tin dichloride) has a bent or V-shaped geometry, which is an example of an angular or non-linear shape. The molecule has a central tin atom that is bonded to two chlorine atoms. The two bond pairs of electrons repel each other, resulting in a bent shape. The bond angle is approximately 119 degrees. This bent shape is due to the lone pair of electrons present on the tin atom. The lone pair occupies a greater amount of space compared to the bond pair, resulting in the distortion of the molecule. In contrast, the linear shape is a molecular geometry in which the atoms of the molecule are arranged in a straight line, like in HCl or CO2. Tetrahedral shape is a molecular geometry that has four atoms attached to a central atom, like in CH4 or CCl4. Trigonal shape is a molecular geometry that has three atoms attached to a central atom, like in BF3.

questions.text: Which statement is true about electron affinity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The value of electron affinity is always positive
options.explanation: Electron affinity is defined as the amount of energy released when an electron is added to a neutral atom to form an anion. Hence, energy is released and cannot be positive

options.text: The value of electron affinity is always negative
options.explanation: Electron affinity cannot always be negative as the first electron affinity is negative but when an electron is added to an already atom that has lost its electron, then the energy is required to add it and the energy is positive this time. Hence, the energy is not always negative.

options.text: The value of first electron affinity is always positive
options.explanation: The value of first electron affinity is always negative as energy is released in adding an electron for the first time.

options.text: The value of first electron affinity is always negative
options.explanation: Whenever an electron is added to a neutral atom, energy is released out so the first electron affinity is always negative. However, when we add an electron to a negatively charged ion, due to the repulsive force between the negative ion and the negative electron, some energy is needed to overcome this repulsion. The energy which is provided is always greater than the energy released hence the second electron affinity is always positive because, ultimately, energy is provided to the system.

questions.explanation: Whenever an electron is added to a neutral atom, energy is released out so the first electron affinity is always negative. However, when we add an electron to a negatively charged ion, due to the repulsive force between the negative ion and the negative electron, some energy is needed to overcome this repulsion. The energy which is provided is always greater than the energy released hence the second electron affinity is always positive because, ultimately, energy is provided to the system.

questions.text: The bond, which is based on attractive forces between oppositely charged ions, is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A covalent bond consists of the mutual sharing of one or more pairs of electrons between two atoms. These electrons are simultaneously attracted by the two atomic nuclei. A covalent bond forms when the difference between the electro negativities of two atoms is too small for an electron transfer to occur to form ions.

In coordination chemistry, a coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond dipolar bond, or coordinate bond is a kind of two-center, two-electron covalent bond in which the two electrons derive from the same atom. It is a form of covalent bond in which the shared pair of electrons comes from a single atom so, option B is also deemed incorrect.

An Ionic bond, also called an electrovalent bond, type of linkage formed from the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions in a chemical compound. Such a bond forms when the valence (outermost) electrons of one atom are transferred permanently to another atom.

Metallic bonds result from the electrostatic attraction between metal cations and delocalized electrons. The nature of metallic bonding accounts for many of the physical properties of metals, such as conductivity and malleability

questions.explanation: An Ionic bond, also called an electrovalent bond, type of linkage formed from the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions in a chemical compound. Such a bond forms when the valence (outermost) electrons of one atom are transferred permanently to another atom.

questions.text: Which statement is incorrect regarding a chemical bond?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Bond is formed by the overlapping of half-filled orbitals
options.explanation: When overlapping of half-filled atomic orbitals takes place along the inter-nuclear axis, the bond formed is called the sigma bond. Hence, the option is correct.

options.text: Bond is formed by the attraction of positive and negative ions
options.explanation: An Ionic bond is formed by the attraction of positive and negative ions. Hence, this option is also correct.

options.text: Bond is formed by the overlapping of “s” orbital is strong
options.explanation: This is also true as the s orbital is the smallest because of which the distance between the outermost electrons and the positively charged nucleus is small, rendering a stronger attractive force.

options.text: Bond formed by the large sized atoms is strong
options.explanation: Bonding tends to take place between the outermost electrons of an atom (or outermost shell). As you increase the atomic size, the electrons that make up an atom begin to fill up higher electron orbitals. These orbitals, which then take place in bonding, are physically further away from the nucleus and thus experience less attraction to it (based on Columb's law). That is the reason why D is an incorrect statement and thus the answer. A is true as the overlapping of half-filled orbitals occurs in covalent bonding; a type of chemical bond. B is true as it occurs in the formation of ionic bonds. C is also true as the s orbital is the smallest orbital because of which the distance between outermost electrons and the positively charged nucleus is small, rendering a stronger attractive force.

questions.explanation: Bonding tends to take place between the outermost electrons of an atom (or outermost shell). As you increase the atomic size, the electrons that make up an atom begin to fill up higher electron orbitals. These orbitals, which then take place in bonding, are physically further away from the nucleus and thus experience less attraction to it (based on Columb's law). That is the reason why D is an incorrect statement and thus the answer. A is true as the overlapping of half-filled orbitals occurs in covalent bonding; a type of chemical bond. B is true as it occurs in the formation of ionic bonds. C is also true as the s orbital is the smallest orbital because of which the distance between outermost electrons and the positively charged nucleus is small, rendering a stronger attractive force.

questions.text: Type of bonding in Sodium (Na) is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metallic bond' is a term used to describe the collective sharing of a sea of valence electrons between several positively charged metal ions. Metallic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that arises from the electrostatic attractive force between conduction electrons and positively charged metal ions. It may be described as the sharing of free electrons among a structure of positively charged ions. All the metal possesses this bond. Na being an alkali metal also possesses a metallic bond.

An ionic bond, also called an electrovalent bond, type of linkage formed from the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions in a chemical compound. Such a bond forms when the valence (outermost) electrons of one atom are transferred permanently to another atom.

A covalent bond consists of the mutual sharing of one or more pairs of electrons between two atoms. These electrons are simultaneously attracted by the two atomic nuclei. A covalent bond forms when the difference between the electro-negativities of two atoms is too small for an electron transfer to occur to form ions. Hence, sodium cannot form covalent bond as sharing of electrons between two sodium atoms won’t complete their octet.

A coordinate bond is a type of alternate covalent bond that is formed by sharing of electron pair from a single atom. Both shared electrons are donated by the same atom. It is also called dative bond or dipolar bond. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The type of bonding in Sodium (Na) is metallic (Option A). 'Metallic bonding' implies the presence of an attractive force between delocalized electrons, initially present in the outermost orbit of the metal atoms, and positively charged metal cations. All metals possess this type of bond. An 'Ionic bond' is formed between a positively charged metal ion and a negatively charged nonmetal ion. A 'Covalent bond' is usually formed between two non-metals. Sodium does not possess a 'coordinate covalent bond' in between its atoms so, all the options are incorrect except Option A.

questions.text: Which of the following Halogens molecules has maximum bond energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: We would expect the B.E of F-F to be the highest but this isn’t so. The reason is that the fluorine atom having the highest electronegativity is smaller in size than the other atoms of its group. Due to this the inter-nuclear repulsive forces operating are larger than others and with a small amount of energy, the bond can be broken down.

Bond energy( B.E ) is a measure of the bond strength of a chemical bond, and is the amount of energy needed to break the atoms involved in a molecular bond into free atoms. B.E. depends on the atomic radius. Going down the group, in halogens, the bond energy decreases. It is because down the group the distance between the outermost electrons and nucleus decreases, decreasing the force of attraction between them. There is an exception in the halogen group which is of fluorine. This is because the atomic radius of fluorine atoms is so small that when two fluorine atoms form an F-F bond, the internuclear repulsive forces between them reduces the bond strength and thus the bond energy. That is why despite being on the top-most position, fluorine has lower bond energy. Where Chlorine is having the least radius thus, its B.E is the highest among all the members of its group.
Bromine’s radius is larger than Cl. As a result, its B.E. is also smaller than Cl. And this option is incorrect.
Iodine’s radius is even larger than bromine, thus having an even smaller B.E than bromine and being the incorrect option.

B.E. depends on the atomic radius. Going down the group, in halogens, the bond energy decreases. It is because down the group the distance between the outermost electrons and nucleus decreases, decreasing the force of attraction between them. There is an exception in the halogen group which is of fluorine. This is because the atomic radius of fluorine atoms is so small that when two fluorine atoms form an F-F bond, the internuclear repulsive forces between them reduces the bond strength and thus the bond energy. That is why despite being on the top-most position, fluorine has lower bond energy. Where Chlorine is having the least radius thus, its B.E is the highest among all the members of its group.

questions.text: Thermal conductivity of metals is due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The layered structure of metals has no effect on thermal conductivity as the conduction through metals is carried out with the assistance of free-moving electrons.

The thermal conductivity of any physical material is a measure of its potential to conduct heat. The freely moving electrons are responsible for the conduction of heat and electricity in the metal. questions.text: In microwave ovens, the wave energy produced is absorbed by certain polar molecules.Which of the following would absorb microwave energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Yes, ethanol (C2H5OH) can absorb microwave energy. Microwave radiation is a form of electromagnetic radiation with wavelengths ranging from about 1 millimeter to 1 meter. When microwave radiation interacts with a substance, it can cause the molecules within the substance to vibrate and rotate, leading to the absorption of the microwave energy. Ethanol, being a polar molecule, has the ability to absorb microwave radiation. The oxygen and hydrogen atoms in the molecule have different electronegativities, resulting in an uneven distribution of charge and the presence of a permanent dipole moment. This polarity allows ethanol molecules to align themselves with the changing electric field of the microwaves, causing them to rotate and absorb energy. As a result, when ethanol is exposed to microwaves, it can heat up as the microwave energy is converted into molecular motion and thermal energy. This is the basis for microwave heating and why substances containing ethanol, such as food or beverages containing alcohol, can heat up when placed in a microwave oven. A polar molecule has a net dipole moment and partial charges in the same molecule due to difference in electronegativity. Option A is correct because ethanol is indeed polar due to the O-H bond, where oxygen being more electronegative pulls the electrons in the shared pair and attains a partial negative charge while hydrogen being less electronegative has a partial positive charge.

No, sodium chloride (NaCl), commonly known as table salt, does not readily absorb microwave energy. Sodium chloride is a polar compound composed of sodium and chloride ions, which do not possess a permanent dipole moment. As a result, sodium chloride molecules do not exhibit the same rotational and vibrational movements in response to microwave radiation as polar molecules like water or ethanol. When exposed to microwave radiation, the majority of sodium chloride molecules do not absorb the energy. Instead, they remain relatively unaffected by the microwaves. This is why solid salt, such as table salt, does not heat up significantly when placed in a microwave oven. However, it's worth noting that while sodium chloride itself may not absorb microwave energy, if it is dissolved in water or present in a mixture with other substances, the water molecules or other polar molecules in the mixture can absorb microwave radiation, leading to heating. In such cases, the heating is primarily due to the polar components rather than the sodium chloride itself. Option B is incorrect because NaCl is ionic and exists as a giant crystal, not a molecule.

Silicon dioxide (SiO2), also known as silica or quartz, does not readily absorb microwave energy. SiO2 is a nonpolar compound composed of silicon and oxygen atoms, and it lacks a permanent dipole moment. Nonpolar compounds like SiO2 do not exhibit strong interactions with microwave radiation. When exposed to microwaves, solid SiO2, such as quartz, typically remains relatively unaffected and does not heat up significantly. Microwaves pass through SiO2 without being absorbed or interacting strongly with the material. However, it's important to note that certain impurities or defects in SiO2 can exhibit different microwave absorption properties. For example, if SiO2 contains trace amounts of metal ions or other impurities, it may exhibit some degree of microwave absorption. Additionally, in certain forms such as powdered or amorphous silica, some absorption may occur due to surface effects or other factors. But in general, pure SiO2 does not absorb microwave energy to a significant extent. C is incorrect because in SiO2 although there is a considerable difference of electronegativity, the dipoles cancel due to the arrangement of atoms. Moreover it has a giant structure, and does not exist as a molecule.

Option D is incorrect because in CH4, both carbon and hydrogen have roughly equal electronegativities and the tendency to attract the shared pairs of electrons.Yes, methane (CH4) can absorb microwave energy. Methane is a nonpolar molecule composed of a central carbon atom bonded to four hydrogen atoms. It does not have a permanent dipole moment and is generally considered nonpolar. However, despite its nonpolar nature, methane can still interact with microwave radiation through a phenomenon known as molecular rotation. Microwaves have the appropriate energy to induce the rotational motion of molecules like methane, causing them to absorb the energy and convert it into thermal energy. Although the absorption of microwaves by methane is relatively weak compared to polar molecules like water or ethanol, it can still absorb a small amount of energy and experience some heating when exposed to microwaves. However, the overall absorption and heating effect of methane in a microwave field are generally less pronounced compared to polar molecules.

questions.explanation: A polar molecule has a net dipole moment and partial charges in the same molecule due to difference in electronegativity. Option A is correct because ethanol is indeed polar due to the O-H bond, where oxygen being more electronegative pulls the electrons in the shared pair and attains a partial negative charge while hydrogen being less electronegative has a partial positive charge.

questions.text: The shape of CO2 molecule is similar to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: No, the shapes of carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen sulfide (H2S) are not similar. They have different molecular geometries. CO2 has a linear molecular geometry, meaning it consists of three atoms arranged in a straight line. The carbon atom is in the center, bonded to two oxygen atoms on either side. The molecule has a bond angle of 180 degrees. H2S, on the other hand, has a bent or V-shaped molecular geometry. It consists of two hydrogen atoms bonded to a sulfur atom. The bond angle in H2S is approximately 92 degrees. So, while both molecules contain two atoms bonded to a central atom, their arrangements in three-dimensional space are different, resulting in distinct shapes. Option A is incorrect because H2S is sp3 hybridised and has an angular shape.

No, the shape of carbon dioxide (CO2) is different from that of sulfur dioxide (SO2). While both molecules have three atoms, they exhibit different molecular geometries. CO2 has a linear molecular geometry, meaning the carbon atom is in the center, bonded to two oxygen atoms on either side. The bond angle in CO2 is 180 degrees. On the other hand, SO2 has a bent or V-shaped molecular geometry. It consists of a sulfur atom bonded to two oxygen atoms. The bond angle in SO2 is approximately 119 degrees. So, despite having similar structures with three atoms, the arrangement of atoms in CO2 and SO2 is different, resulting in distinct molecular shapes. Option B is incorrect because SO2 is sp2 hybridised and has trigonal planar shape.

No, the shape of tin(II) chloride (SnCl2) is different from that of carbon dioxide (CO2). SnCl2 has a bent or V-shaped molecular geometry. It consists of a central tin (Sn) atom bonded to two chlorine (Cl) atoms. The bond angle in SnCl2 is approximately 95 degrees. In contrast, CO2 has a linear molecular geometry, with a carbon (C) atom in the center bonded to two oxygen (O) atoms on either side. The bond angle in CO2 is 180 degrees. So, the molecular shapes of SnCl2 and CO2 differ significantly. SnCl2 is bent, while CO2 is linear. Option C is incorrect because SnCl2 is sp2 hybridised and exists as a crystalline ionic solid.

No, the shape of carbon dioxide (CO2) is different from that of beryllium fluoride (BeF2). CO2 has a linear molecular geometry, consisting of a central carbon (C) atom bonded to two oxygen (O) atoms on opposite sides. The bond angle in CO2 is 180 degrees. On the other hand, BeF2 has a linear molecular geometry as well. It consists of a central beryllium (Be) atom bonded to two fluorine (F) atoms on either side. The bond angle in BeF2 is also 180 degrees. Both CO2 and BeF2 have a linear shape, with the central atom bonded to two atoms on opposite sides. So, in terms of molecular geometry, CO2 and BeF2 are similar as they both exhibit linear shapes. CO2 is sp hybridised, has a linear structure and a bond angle of 180°. BeF2 is also sp hybridised and has the same structure and bond angles as CO2, since the central atom Be, like carbon, bonds with two other atoms and has no lone pairs. Therefore option D is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: CO2 is sp hybridised, has a linear structure and a bond angle of 180°. BeF2 is also sp hybridised and has the same structure and bond angles as CO2, since the central atom Be, like carbon, bonds with two other atoms and has no lone pairs. Therefore option D is the correct answer.

questions.text: For the formation of ionic bond, electronegativity difference should be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Zero electronegativity difference results in the formation of non-polar covalent bond.
0.5 electronegativity difference also results in the formation of non-polar covalent bond.

Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract electrons towards itself in a chemical bond. For the formation of an ionic bond, there should be a significant difference in electronegativity between the participating atoms. Typically, an electronegativity difference of 1.7 or greater on the Pauling scale is considered indicative of an ionic bond. In other words, if the electronegativity difference between two atoms is equal to or greater than 1.7, it suggests that one atom has a much stronger attraction for electrons than the other.
Less than 1.7 electronegativity difference results in the formation of polar covalent bond.

questions.explanation: Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract electrons towards itself in a chemical bond. For the formation of an ionic bond, there should be a significant difference in electronegativity between the participating atoms. Typically, an electronegativity difference of 1.7 or greater on the Pauling scale is considered indicative of an ionic bond. In other words, if the electronegativity difference between two atoms is equal to or greater than 1.7, it suggests that one atom has a much stronger attraction for electrons than the other.

questions.text: The hydration energy of Mg2+ is less than:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The hydration energy of Mg2+ is generally greater than that of Na+. Hydration energy refers to the energy released when ions are surrounded by water molecules and solvated. It is influenced by factors such as the charge and size of the ion. Mg2+ has a larger charge (+2) compared to Na+ (+1), which means that it has a stronger attractive force towards water molecules. The higher charge of Mg2+ leads to stronger electrostatic interactions between the Mg2+ ion and water molecules, resulting in a higher hydration energy. Additionally, the smaller size of Mg2+ compared to Na+ allows water molecules to come closer to the ion, leading to stronger hydration interactions. Therefore, in general, the hydration energy of Mg2+ is higher than that of Na+. Option A is incorrect because Na+ has a smaller charge.

The hydration energy of Mg2+ is generally higher than that of Ca2+. The hydration energy of an ion is influenced by factors such as the charge and size of the ion. Both Mg2+ and Ca2+ have a charge of +2, but the size of the ions differs. Mg2+ is smaller in size compared to Ca2+. The smaller size of Mg2+ allows water molecules to come closer to the ion, resulting in stronger hydration interactions. The stronger electrostatic interactions between Mg2+ and water molecules lead to a higher hydration energy. On the other hand, Ca2+ is larger in size compared to Mg2+. The larger size of Ca2+ restricts the approach of water molecules, leading to weaker hydration interactions and a relatively lower hydration energy compared to Mg2+. Therefore, in general, the hydration energy of Mg2+ is higher than that of Ca2+. Option B is incorrect because Ca2+ has a larger radius.

The hydration energy of Mg2+ is generally greater than that of Li+. The hydration energy of an ion is influenced by factors such as the charge and size of the ion. Mg2+ has a charge of +2, while Li+ has a charge of +1. The higher charge of Mg2+ means that it has a stronger attractive force towards water molecules compared to Li+. As a result, the hydration energy of Mg2+ is generally higher. Furthermore, the smaller size of Mg2+ allows water molecules to come closer to the ion, leading to stronger hydration interactions. In contrast, the larger size of Li+ restricts the approach of water molecules, resulting in weaker hydration interactions and a lower hydration energy compared to Mg2+. Therefore, in general, the hydration energy of Mg2+ is higher than that of Li+. Option C is incorrect because Li+ has a smaller charge.

The hydration energy of Mg2+ is generally greater than that of Al3+. The hydration energy of an ion is influenced by factors such as the charge and size of the ion. Both Mg2+ and Al3+ have a charge of +2 and +3, respectively. The higher charge of Al3+ compared to Mg2+ means that Al3+ has a stronger attractive force towards water molecules. This stronger electrostatic interaction leads to a higher hydration energy for Al3+. Additionally, the smaller size of Mg2+ compared to Al3+ allows water molecules to come closer to the ion, resulting in stronger hydration interactions. The larger size of Al3+ restricts the approach of water molecules, leading to weaker hydration interactions and a relatively lower hydration energy compared to Mg2+. Therefore, in general, the hydration energy of Mg2+ is higher than that of Al3+. The hydration energy is the energy released when a gaseous ion is dissolved in excess water to form an infinitely dilute solution. It is always exothermic because heat is liberated when attractive bonds form between water molecules and the ions. Hydration energy is directly proportional to the charge density, so an ion with the greatest magnitude of charge and smallest radius will have the highest hydration enthalpy. Al3+ has a charge greater than Mg2+ and a radius slightly smaller than it, therefore it has a greater value of hydration energy and hence, the correct answer is D.

questions.explanation: The hydration energy is the energy released when a gaseous ion is dissolved in excess water to form an infinitely dilute solution. It is always exothermic because heat is liberated when attractive bonds form between water molecules and the ions. Hydration energy is directly proportional to the charge density, so an ion with the greatest magnitude of charge and smallest radius will have the highest hydration enthalpy. Al3+ has a charge greater than Mg2+ and a radius slightly smaller than it, therefore it has a greater value of hydration energy and hence, the correct answer is D.

questions.text: Which of the following is the worst conductor of electricity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because Hg being a metal has a metallic lattice whereby there is a sea of electrons that enable it to conduct electricity. Mercury (Hg) is not the worst conductor of electricity, but it is a poor conductor. It is classified as a metal, and as such, it does conduct electricity to some extent. However, compared to other metals like copper or silver, mercury has a relatively high electrical resistance. This means that it is less efficient at conducting electricity. Additionally, mercury is a liquid at room temperature, which further limits its practical use as a conductor. Therefore, while mercury is not the worst conductor overall, it is not commonly used or considered among the best conductors of electricity.

Pure distilled water is a poor conductor of electricity. Water itself is a good electrical insulator, but it can conduct electricity when it contains dissolved ions or impurities. These ions, such as sodium (Na+), chloride (Cl-), or other dissolved salts, increase the conductivity of water by allowing the flow of electric current. In order to conduct electricity, a substance must possess charged particles, either ions or electrons. Distilled water has neither of those as it does not dissociate into H+ or OH- ions unless it is dissolved with some other polar substance. Hence it does not conduct electricity and therefore option B is the correct answer.

No, NaCl (sodium chloride), when dissolved in water or in molten form, is actually a good conductor of electricity. When NaCl dissolves in water, it dissociates into sodium ions (Na+) and chloride ions (Cl-), which are responsible for conducting the electric current. The presence of these ions in solution allows for the movement of charged particles, facilitating the flow of electricity. As a result, NaCl solution (commonly known as saltwater) can conduct electricity relatively well compared to pure water or other non-ionic substances. Option C is incorrect because NaCl being an ionic compound, has free ions in the liquid state thus possessing free ions to conduct electricity.

A dilute sugar solution, in general, is a poor conductor of electricity. Sugar, or sucrose, is a non-ionic compound, which means it does not readily dissociate into charged particles (ions) when dissolved in water. As a result, a dilute sugar solution does not contain enough ions to facilitate the conduction of electric current.While water molecules can interact with sugar molecules and form weak hydrogen bonds, these interactions do not result in the formation of free-moving charged particles that can conduct electricity. Therefore, compared to ionic compounds like NaCl, a dilute sugar solution has significantly lower conductivity. It's important to note that highly concentrated sugar solutions or solutions that contain other ionic impurities can exhibit some level of electrical conductivity. However, in general, a dilute sugar solution would be considered a poor conductor of electricity. Option D could also be correct since sugar solution, although polar, does not possess any free ions so it cannot conduct electricity. However, in such questions, it is best to prefer water as the first choice.

questions.explanation: Pure distilled water is a poor conductor of electricity. Water itself is a good electrical insulator, but it can conduct electricity when it contains dissolved ions or impurities. These ions, such as sodium (Na+), chloride (Cl-), or other dissolved salts, increase the conductivity of water by allowing the flow of electric current. In order to conduct electricity, a substance must possess charged particles, either ions or electrons. Distilled water has neither of those as it does not dissociate into H+ or OH- ions unless it is dissolved with some other polar substance. Hence it does not conduct electricity and therefore option B is the correct answer.

questions.text: Linear shaped hybrid orbital is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Linear-shaped hybrid orbitals are formed by the combination of one s orbital and one p orbital. The resulting hybrid orbital is called an sp orbital. The angle between the two orbitals is 180°. Examples of molecules that contain sp hybrid orbitals include acetylene (C2H2) and carbon monoxide (CO).

The hybrid orbital designated as "sp2" refers to a trigonal planar-shaped hybrid orbital. It is formed when one s orbital and two p orbitals from the same atom combine to form three hybrid orbitals. The sp2 hybridization occurs when an atom is involved in three sigma bonds and has one lone pair of electrons. The resulting trigonal planar geometry allows for a planar arrangement of bonding and non-bonding electron pairs around the central atom.

The hybrid orbital designated as "sp3" refers to a tetrahedral-shaped hybrid orbital. It is formed when one s orbital and three p orbitals from the same atom combine to form four hybrid orbitals. The sp3 hybridization occurs when an atom is involved in four sigma bonds and has no lone pairs of electrons. The resulting tetrahedral geometry allows for a tetrahedral arrangement of bonding and non-bonding electron pairs around the central atom.
The designation "dsp" is not commonly used to describe hybrid orbitals. It does not represent a specific hybridization state or shape of orbitals.

questions.explanation: Linear-shaped hybrid orbitals are formed by the combination of one s orbital and one p orbital. The resulting hybrid orbital is called an sp orbital. The angle between the two orbitals is 180°. Examples of molecules that contain sp hybrid orbitals include acetylene (C2H2) and carbon monoxide (CO).

This angle corresponds to a trigonal planar geometry, not a tetrahedral geometry.
This angle corresponds to a square planar or square pyramidal geometry, not a tetrahedral geometry.

questions.text: s - sp3 overlap occurs in _ molecule.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cl3- is actually an ion Which ahead makes bond with another ion We usually go through lewis structure of this ion.

Hybridization is the idea that atomic orbitals fuse to form newly hybridized orbitals, which in turn, influences molecular geometry and bonding properties. When we talk about CH4 it is basically a combination of 1 carbon and 4 hydrogen atoms. However, to form this compound the central atom carbon which has 4 valence electrons obtain more electrons from 4 hydrogen atoms to complete its octet. When the electrons are shared between carbon and hydrogen there is a formation of a covalent bond or bonds to be more accurate. Now coming to the hybridization of methane, the central atom carbon is sp3 hybridized. This is because one 2s orbital and three 2p orbitals in the valence shell of carbon combine to form four sp3 hybrid orbitals which are of equal energy and shape. Further, four H atoms also use these four sp3 hybrid orbitals of carbon to form C-H sigma bonds which ultimately leads to the formation of the methane molecule.
There is no hybridization in HF. Pure 2p orbital of fluorine interacts with 1s of hydrogen and forms a covalent bond. Moreover, at least 3 atoms (or two bonds) are required to draw an angle.

questions.explanation: The methane molecule has four equal bonds. In hybridization, carbon's 2s and three 2p orbitals combine into four identical orbitals, now called sp3 hybrids.

questions.text: Select amongst the following which is not correct:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Nitrogen is poor conductor of heat and electricity.
options.explanation: Nitrogen is poor conductor of heat and electricity. This statement is correct. Nitrogen is a non-metal, and non-metals are generally poor conductors of heat and electricity. This is because non-metals have high ionization energies, which means that they do not easily give up electrons.

Nitrogen only gives acidic oxides. This statement is not correct. Nitrogen can also form neutral and basic oxides. For example, nitrogen monoxide (NO) is a neutral oxide, while ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH) is a basic oxide.

options.text: Nitrogen has greatest tendency to attract electrons
options.explanation: Nitrogen has greatest tendency to attract electrons. This statement is correct. Nitrogen has a higher electronegativity than most other elements, which means that it has a greater tendency to attract electrons. This is why nitrogen forms predominantly covalent compounds.

The compounds are predominantly covalent. This statement is correct. Nitrogen forms predominantly covalent compounds because it has a high electronegativity. Covalent compounds are formed when two atoms share electrons. The more electronegative an atom is, the more strongly it will attract the shared electrons. In the case of nitrogen, the shared electrons are strongly attracted to the nitrogen atom, which makes the compound predominantly covalent.

questions.text: Select amongst the following which is not a correct statement for the element it is referring to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Nitrogen is poor conductor of heat and electricity.
options.explanation: Nitrogen is poor conductor of heat and electricity. This statement is correct. Nitrogen is a non-metal, and non-metals are generally poor conductors of heat and electricity. This is because non-metals have high ionization energies, which means that they do not easily give up electrons.

Nitrogen only gives acidic oxides. This statement is not correct. Nitrogen can also form neutral and basic oxides. For example, nitrogen monoxide (NO) is a neutral oxide, while ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH) is a basic oxide.

options.text: Nitrogen has greatest tendency to attract electrons
options.explanation: Nitrogen does not have the greatest tendency to attract electrons among all the elements. On the periodic table, elements such as fluorine (F) and oxygen (O) have higher electronegativity values compared to nitrogen (N). Fluorine is the most electronegative element, with the highest tendency to attract electrons. Oxygen, which is just above nitrogen in the periodic table, also has a higher electronegativity.

options.text: The compounds formed by nitrogen are predominantly covalent
options.explanation: The compounds are predominantly covalent. This statement is correct. Nitrogen forms predominantly covalent compounds because it has a high electronegativity. Covalent compounds are formed when two atoms share electrons. The more electronegative an atom is, the more strongly it will attract the shared electrons. In the case of nitrogen, the shared electrons are strongly attracted to the nitrogen atom, which makes the compound predominantly covalent.

questions.explanation: Nitrogen only gives acidic oxides. This statement is not correct. Nitrogen can also form neutral and basic oxides. For example, nitrogen monoxide (NO) is a neutral oxide, while ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH) is a basic oxide.

Hybridization is the idea that atomic orbitals fuse to form newly hybridized orbitals, which in turn, influences molecular geometry and bonding properties. When we talk about CH4 it is basically a combination of 1 carbon and 4 hydrogen atoms. However, to form this compound the central atom carbon which has 4 valence electrons obtain more electrons from 4 hydrogen atoms to complete its octet. When the electrons are shared between carbon and hydrogen there is a formation of a covalent bond or bonds to be more accurate. Now coming to the hybridization of methane, the central atom carbon is sp3 hybridized. This is because one 2s orbital and three 2p orbitals in the valence shell of carbon combine to form four sp3 hybrid orbitals which are of equal energy and shape. Further, four H atoms also use these four sp3 hybrid orbitals of carbon to form C-H sigma bonds which ultimately leads to the formation of the methane molecule.
There is no hybridization in HF. Pure 2p orbital of fluorine interacts with 1s of hydrogen and forms a covalent bond. Moreover, at least 3 atoms (or two bonds) are required to draw an angle.

The relative tendency of a bonded atom in a molecule to attract the shared electron pair towards itself is called electronegativity. The positively charged protons in the nucleus attract the negatively charged electrons. As the number of protons in the nucleus increases, the electronegativity or attraction will increase. questions.text: The metallic lustre exhibited by sodium is explained by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect answer.

Sodium is an element with symbol Na and atomic number 11. Sodium is a metal. Metallic lustre of sodium is because of the oscillation of loosely bound electrons. When light falls on the surface of the metal, free electrons in the metal get excited to higher energy levels and when it returns back to the lower energy levels, it emits light. Metals have a bright physical appearance. Metals are shiny and lustrous. The colour of solid sodium is silvery-white. The free sodium metal does not occur in an environment prepared or obtained from its compounds. Sodium is an alkali metal which is present in group 1 of the periodic table, as it has one valence electron. The atomic mass of sodium is 23. Sodium metal exists in various minerals such as rock salt, feldspar and sodalite. Sodium ions are soluble ions in aqueous solutions.

questions.text: The hybridization of carbon in diamond, graphite and acetylene is respectively:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refer to the main text for explanation.
Refer to the main text for explanation.

The hybridisation of carbon atom in diamond, graphite and acetylene are respectively sp3, sp2, sp. In diamond, each carbon combine with 4 other carbon atoms with four sigma bonds. Hence, the hybridization of carbon is sp3. In graphite, each carbon combines with 3 other carbon atoms with three sigma bonds. so sp2 hybridization. In acetylene, one carbon combines with another carbon atom with three bonds (1 sigma and 2 pi bonds). so sp2 hybridization.

questions.text: Melting and boiling point of transition elements is higher due to?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Binding energy refers to the energy required to disassemble a whole system into separate parts. In the context of transition metals, this refers to the energy needed to separate the atoms in the metal. Transition metals have higher binding energy due to the presence of more unpaired electrons in their d-orbitals, which can form more bonds and thus require more energy to break. Hence, their boiling and melting points are high

Transition elements are characterized by their partially filled d orbitals, which provide them with unique properties, including higher melting and boiling points. Here's why: 1. Presence of Unpaired d Electrons: Transition elements have incompletely filled d orbitals in their electron configuration. These unpaired electrons contribute to the formation of strong metallic bonds between the atoms in the crystal lattice. Metallic bonds are formed due to the delocalization of electrons within the crystal structure, leading to strong attractive forces between the metal atoms. 2. Increased Effective Nuclear Charge: The unpaired d electrons experience a higher effective nuclear charge (the net positive charge experienced by an electron in a multi-electron atom) compared to the fully filled d orbitals in non-transition elements. This increased effective nuclear charge enhances the attraction between the nucleus and the valence electrons, contributing to the higher melting and boiling points. 3. Stronger London Dispersion Forces: Transition elements have more extensive electron clouds due to the presence of the d electrons. As a result, the London dispersion forces, which are attractive forces between temporary dipoles induced by fluctuations in electron distribution, are stronger in transition elements. These forces also contribute to the higher melting and boiling points. 4. Increased Electron Density: The presence of unpaired d electrons results in higher electron density in the crystal lattice. This increased electron density leads to stronger electron-electron repulsions, which in turn contribute to the higher melting and boiling points.

Hardness is a measure of how resistant a material is to various kinds of permanent shape changes when a compressive force is applied. While hardness does correlate with high melting and boiling points, it's not a direct cause. However, transition metals are generally harder due to the same reasons that give them higher melting and boiling points (i.e., more bonding opportunities from unpaired d-orbital electrons).

This option suggests that all the above factors contribute to the higher melting and boiling points of transition metals. While A and B are directly responsible, C (hardness) is more of a correlated property rather than a cause. So, while all these factors are associated with the properties of transition metals, only A and B are the direct causes of higher melting and boiling points.

questions.text: The smallest part of the crystal lattice has all the properties of the entire crystal, this is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The unit cell is the smallest repeating structure in a crystal lattice that, when repeated in three dimensions, reproduces the entire crystal's pattern. It possesses the same properties as the overall crystal. Therefore, this option is not correct.

The smallest part of a crystal lattice that has all the properties of the entire crystal is called a "unit cell." A unit cell is a repeating structural unit that, when stacked together in three dimensions, forms the entire crystal lattice. The arrangement of atoms, ions, or molecules within the unit cell represents the crystal's basic building block, and this pattern repeats throughout the entire crystal structure. Properties such as symmetry, atomic arrangement, and density observed in the crystal are reflected in the unit cell. The unit cell's dimensions and angles are used to describe the crystal's lattice parameters, which define the overall structure and properties of the crystal. There are several types of unit cells, including simple cubic, body-centered cubic, face-centered cubic, and more complex unit cells in more complex crystal structures. The type of unit cell a crystal possesses depends on the arrangement of its constituent particles and the crystal system to which it belongs. By studying the unit cell and its properties, scientists and researchers can gain insights into the overall crystal structure and understand the material's physical, mechanical, and chemical behavior.

questions.text: How do the 'p' orbitals px, py, pz differ from each other?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The 'p' orbitals (px, py, pz) differ in their orientation in space: 1. **px Orbital:** This orbital is aligned along the x-axis. 2. **py Orbital:** This orbital is aligned along the y-axis. 3. **pz Orbital:** This orbital is aligned along the z-axis. They have the same energy and shape but differ in their spatial orientation. Hence, this option is not correct.

The 'p' orbitals (px, py, pz) differ in their orientation in space: 1. **px Orbital:** This orbital is aligned along the x-axis. 2. **py Orbital:** This orbital is aligned along the y-axis. 3. **pz Orbital:** This orbital is aligned along the z-axis. They have the same energy and shape but differ in their spatial orientation. Hence, this option is not correct.

The 'p' orbitals (px, py, and pz) are a set of three degenerate orbitals that belong to the second energy level (n=2) in an atom. These orbitals have different orientations in space, and they are a part of the electron cloud surrounding the nucleus. Here's how the 'p' orbitals differ from each other: 1. **Orientation in Space:** - px orbital: It is aligned along the x-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system, which means it extends in the horizontal plane. - py orbital: It is aligned along the y-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system, which means it extends in the vertical plane. - pz orbital: It is aligned along the z-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system, which means it extends in the direction perpendicular to both the x and y axes (i.e., it points along the vertical axis). 2. **Shape:** - All three 'p' orbitals have a dumbbell shape with a node (region of zero probability of finding an electron) at the nucleus and two lobes of opposite phase (one positive and one negative) on either side of the node. These lobes represent the regions of high probability of finding the electron. 3. **Degeneracy:** - The 'p' orbitals have the same energy, making them degenerate orbitals. This means that they have identical energy levels in a single atom. 4. **Quantum Numbers:** - Each 'p' orbital is defined by three quantum numbers: n (principal quantum number), l (azimuthal quantum number), and ml (magnetic quantum number). - For the 'p' orbitals: n=2, l=1, and ml can take values of -1, 0, and +1, corresponding to the px, py, and pz orbitals, respectively. It's important to note that 'p' orbitals can hold up to two electrons each (a total of six electrons for all three 'p' orbitals in an atom). These orbitals play a crucial role in the formation of chemical bonds and contribute significantly to the molecular geometry and bonding properties of molecules.

They have the same energy and shape but differ in their spatial orientation. Hence, this option is not correct.

questions.text: If a molecule contains 4 electron pairs, the shape of molecule will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: If a molecule contains 4 electron pairs, its shape will be **tetrahedral**. In chemistry, the shape of a molecule is determined by its molecular geometry, which depends on the arrangement of its bonding and non-bonding electron pairs. The number of electron pairs around the central atom is determined by the molecule's steric number, which is the sum of the number of bonded atoms and lone pairs on the central atom. In the case of a molecule with 4 electron pairs, the possible arrangements are: 1. **Tetrahedral**: This shape occurs when there are 4 bonding pairs around the central atom, and there are no lone pairs. The bond angles in a tetrahedral molecule are approximately 109.5 degrees. 2. **Trigonal Pyramidal**: This shape occurs when there are 3 bonding pairs and 1 lone pair around the central atom. 3. **Bent**: This shape occurs when there are 2 bonding pairs and 2 lone pairs around the central atom. It's important to note that the steric number and the shape of a molecule can provide valuable information about its physical and chemical properties, as well as its reactivity with other molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following forces have no relation with valence electrons?

options.explanation: Ionic bonds are a type of chemical bond that involves the transfer of valence electrons between atoms, resulting in the formation of oppositely charged ions.

Chemical bonds are the forces that hold atoms together in a compound, including covalent bonds, ionic bonds, and metallic bonds. These bonds involve the sharing, transfer, or pooling of valence electrons.

An intermolecular force (IMF) (or secondary force) is the force that mediates the interaction between molecules, including the electromagnetic forces of attraction or repulsion which act between atoms and other types of neighbouring particles, e.g. atoms or ions. Intermolecular forces are weak relative to intramolecular forces - the forces which hold a molecule together. For example, the covalent bond, involving sharing electron pairs between atoms, is much stronger than the forces present between the neighbouring molecules.
An intramolecular force is any force that binds together the atoms making up a molecule or compound, not to be confused with intermolecular forces, which are the forces present between molecules.

It explains all the properties that are related to the microscopic phenomenon.

questions.explanation: The kinetic theory of gases is a theoretical model that describes the molecular composition of the gas in terms of a large number of submicroscopic particles which include atoms and molecules. Furthermore, the theory explains that gas pressure arises due to particles colliding with each other and the walls of the container. The kinetic theory of gases also defines properties such as temperature, volume, and pressure and as well as transport properties such as viscosity and thermal conductivity. It explains all the properties that are related to the microscopic phenomenon.

questions.text: The increase in size of an anion is due to the increase in repulsion of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electron-proton is not the correct answer as the number of protons in the nucleus remains the same when an atom gains an electron to become an anion.

In general, anions are larger than the corresponding neutral atom, since adding electrons increases the number of electron-electron repulsion interactions that take place. Cations are smaller than the corresponding neutral atoms, since the valence electrons, which are furthest away from the nucleus, are lost.

Electron-nucleus is also not the correct answer as the attraction between the negatively charged electrons and positively charged nucleus actually increases when an atom gains an electron to become an anion.
Proton-nucleus is not the correct answer as the number of protons in the nucleus does not change when an atom gains an electron to become an anion.

Cations are smaller than the corresponding neutral atoms, since the valence electrons, which are furthest away from the nucleus, are lost.Option C: Electron-nucleus is also not the correct answer as the attraction between the negatively charged electrons and positively charged nucleus increases when an atom gains an electron to become an anion.Option D: Proton-nucleus is not the correct answer as the number of protons in the nucleus does not change when an atom gains an electron to become an anion.
hints: increase in size of an anion

questions.text: Non-polar covalent bond is formed between:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Non-polar covalent bonds form between two atoms of the same element or between different elements that share the electrons equally. For example, an oxygen atom can bond with another oxygen atom to fill its outer shell.

If the two atoms in a covalent bond have different electronegativities, the electron pair is pulled closer to the more electronegative atom, resulting in a polar covalent bond. Examples of heteronuclear diatomic molecules include HCl, CO, and HF.

The term "similar atoms" is vague and doesn't provide enough information to determine whether a bond would be polar or nonpolar. If the atoms have similar electronegativities, a nonpolar covalent bond may be formed, but if they have different electronegativities, a polar covalent bond would result.
If the atoms in a covalent bond are dissimilar and have different electronegativities, the bond is likely to be polar covalent rather than nonpolar.

options.explanation: Two polar molecules coming closer is incorrect because polar molecules already have a permanent dipole moment due to the unequal distribution of electrons in the molecule. The interaction between polar molecules is due to dipole-dipole interactions, not instantaneous dipole-induced dipole interactions.

Because of the constant motion of the electrons, an atom or molecule can develop a temporary (instantaneous) dipole when its electrons are distributed unsymmetrically about the nucleus. questions.text: When two atoms are bonded to form a stable compound, the attractive forces will _ repulsive forces and the potential energy of the system will be _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Recessive, higher is incorrect because the attractive forces between the atoms are dominant over the repulsive forces, leading to a lower potential energy and a stable compound.

To form a stable compound, corresponding atoms must have higher attractive forces, that can outclass the repulsive forces. If the attractive forces are greater and a compound is formed, it won't be having any potential energy since all that energy was used up in bringing the atoms together in the first place.
Dominate, maximum is incorrect because the repulsive forces would not dominate over the attractive forces in a stable chemical bond.
Recessive, lower is incorrect because the potential energy of the system decreases when the attractive forces dominate over the repulsive forces, resulting in a more stable compound.
