questions.text: Type of bonding in Sodium (Na) is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metallic bond is a term used to describe the collective sharing of a sea of valence electrons between several positively charged metal ions. Metallic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that arises from the electrostatic attractive force between conduction electrons and positively charged metal ions. It may be described as the sharing of free electrons among a structure of positively charged ions. All the metal possesses this bond. Na being an alkali metal also possesses a metallic bond.

An ionic bond, also called an electrovalent bond, type of linkage formed from the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions in a chemical compound. Such a bond forms when the valence (outermost) electrons of one atom are transferred permanently to another atom.

A covalent bond consists of the mutual sharing of one or more pairs of electrons between two atoms. These electrons are simultaneously attracted by the two atomic nuclei. A covalent bond forms when the difference between the electro-negativities of two atoms is too small for an electron transfer to occur to form ions. Hence, sodium cannot form covalent bond as sharing of electrons between two sodium atoms won’t complete their octet.

A coordinate bond is a type of alternate covalent bond that is formed by sharing of electron pair from a single atom. Both shared electrons are donated by the same atom. It is also called dative bond or dipolar bond. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The type of bonding in Sodium (Na) is metallic (Option A). 'Metallic bonding' implies the presence of an attractive force between delocalized electrons, initially present in the outermost orbit of the metal atoms, and positively charged metal cations. All metals possess this type of bond. An 'Ionic bond' is formed between a positively charged metal ion and a negatively charged nonmetal ion. A 'Covalent bond' is usually formed between two non-metals. Sodium does not possess a 'coordinate covalent bond' in between its atoms so, all the options are incorrect except Option A.

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3 is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is a molecule or ion that can accept electrons. Acceptor species are typically electron-deficient, meaning they have a valence shell that is not completely filled with electrons. This makes them capable of accepting electrons from other molecules or ions. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B .The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

A neutral species is an atom or molecule that has a net charge of zero. This means that the number of electrons in the atom or molecule is equal to the number of protons in the nucleus. Neutral species are the most common type of atom or molecule in the universe. They are found in all three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas. Neutral species are also the building blocks of all more complex molecules.

A negatively charged particle is called an anion. An anion is formed when an atom gains one or more electrons. The extra electrons make the atom negatively charged. An atom can gain electrons by reacting with a more electropositive atom. Electropositive atoms are atoms that are more likely to lose electrons. For example, a chlorine atom can gain one electron from a sodium atom to form the chloride ion (Cl-).

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

questions.text: Which of the following ways can an atom use to attain stability?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Exactly, atoms achieve stability by gaining, losing, or sharing electrons. These processes involve interactions between atoms that lead to a more stable electron configuration, typically resembling the electron arrangement of noble gases. - **Gaining Electrons (Anions):** Atoms can gain electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become negatively charged ions (anions). - **Losing Electrons (Cations):** Atoms can lose electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become positively charged ions (cations). - **Sharing Electrons (Covalent Bonds):** Atoms can share electrons with other atoms to achieve a stable electron configuration through covalent bonding. This is common in nonmetals. These electron interactions allow atoms to satisfy the octet rule (or duet rule for hydrogen and helium), leading to increased stability. Therefore this option is correct.

These processes involve interactions between atoms that lead to a more stable electron configuration, typically resembling the electron arrangement of noble gases. - **Gaining Electrons (Anions):** Atoms can gain electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become negatively charged ions (anions). - **Losing Electrons (Cations):** Atoms can lose electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become positively charged ions (cations). - **Sharing Electrons (Covalent Bonds):** Atoms can share electrons with other atoms to achieve a stable electron configuration through covalent bonding. This is common in nonmetals. These electron interactions allow atoms to satisfy the octet rule (or duet rule for hydrogen and helium), leading to increased stability. Therefore this option is correct.

These processes involve interactions between atoms that lead to a more stable electron configuration, typically resembling the electron arrangement of noble gases. - **Gaining Electrons (Anions):** Atoms can gain electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become negatively charged ions (anions). - **Losing Electrons (Cations):** Atoms can lose electrons to achieve a full outer electron shell and become positively charged ions (cations). - **Sharing Electrons (Covalent Bonds):** Atoms can share electrons with other atoms to achieve a stable electron configuration through covalent bonding. This is common in nonmetals. These electron interactions allow atoms to satisfy the octet rule (or duet rule for hydrogen and helium), leading to increased stability. Therefore this option is correct.
As all A, B and C options are correct so option D is right option.

questions.explanation: An atom is most stable when its outermost shell is filled with electrons. Thus in order to gain stability, an atom may gain, lose or share electrons to fill its outermost shell with electrons.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has six bonding electrons?

questions.text: If two atoms are bonded in such a way that one member of the covalently bonded molecule donates both electrons that are shared, then what is this type of bond called?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding involves a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly electronegative atom (such as oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom from a neighboring molecule. It's a special type of dipole-dipole interaction, not characterized by one atom donating both electrons in a bond, hence this is not the correct option.

A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons. In a typical covalent bond, both atoms contribute electron to form the shared pair. The scenario where one atom donates both electrons is specifically referred to as a "coordinate covalent bond." Hence this option is incorrect.

This is the correct term for the type of bond described in the question. A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond or a Lewis acid-base bond, is formed when one atom (the donor) provides both electrons for sharing with another atom (the acceptor). This type of bond is often indicated by an arrow pointing from the donor atom to the acceptor atom.

An electrovalent bond is another term for an ionic bond. Ionic bonds are formed between atoms with significantly different electronegativities, resulting in the transfer of electrons from one atom to another to form positively and negatively charged ions, hence this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond or a Lewis acid-base bond, is formed when one atom (the donor) provides both electrons for sharing with another atom (the acceptor). This type of bond is often indicated by an arrow pointing from the donor atom to the acceptor atom.

questions.text: The distance between the nuclei of two atoms forming a covalent bond is called the bond length. Which of the following factors affect the bond lenght?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The greater the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms, the shorter the bond length. This is because the more electronegative atom will have a greater pull on the shared electrons, which will bring the two atoms closer together.
The larger the atoms, the longer the bond length. This is because the larger atoms have larger electron clouds, which means that the shared electrons are more spread out.
The greater the bond order, the shorter the bond length. This is because a higher bond order means that there are more shared electrons, which brings the two atoms closer together.
All above explanation.

questions.text: _ is also known as a dative bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not also known as a dative bond. An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal. In an ionic bond, the metal donates one or more electrons to the nonmetal, which results in the formation of oppositely charged ions. The metal ion is positively charged, while the nonmetal ion is negatively charged. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, which holds the molecule together.

No, covalent bond is not also known as a dative bond. A covalent bond is a type of chemical bond where two atoms share a pair of electrons. The electrons are shared equally between the two atoms. A dative bond is also a type of covalent bond, but it is a special type of covalent bond where one atom donates both electrons to the other.

A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

No, metallic bond and dative bond are two different types of bonds. Metallic bond is a type of chemical bond that holds together metal atoms in a solid. It is formed by the delocalized electrons of the metal atoms. The delocalized electrons are free to move throughout the metal, which gives metals their characteristic properties, such as conductivity, malleability, and ductility. Dative bond (also called coordinate bond) is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons in the shared pair come from the same atom. It is formed when an atom with a lone pair of electrons donates that pair to an atom that is electron deficient.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct. A coordinate covalent bond is also known as a dative bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which both electrons are donated by the same atom. This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3 is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is a molecule or ion that can accept electrons. Acceptor species are typically electron-deficient, meaning they have a valence shell that is not completely filled with electrons. This makes them capable of accepting electrons from other molecules or ions. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B .The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

A neutral species is an atom or molecule that has a net charge of zero. This means that the number of electrons in the atom or molecule is equal to the number of protons in the nucleus. Neutral species are the most common type of atom or molecule in the universe. They are found in all three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas. Neutral species are also the building blocks of all more complex molecules.

A negatively charged particle is called an anion. An anion is formed when an atom gains one or more electrons. The extra electrons make the atom negatively charged. An atom can gain electrons by reacting with a more electropositive atom. Electropositive atoms are atoms that are more likely to lose electrons. For example, a chlorine atom can gain one electron from a sodium atom to form the chloride ion (Cl-).

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom.

questions.text: Which of the following hydrated salts most likely exists at normal temperature?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lithium is the smallest in size among the alkali metals. Hence, Li+ ion can polarize water molecules more easily than other alkali metals. As a result, water molecules get attached to lithium salts as water of crystallization.

Sodium chloride, or table salt, readily dissolves in water. At room temperature (normal temperatures), it forms a homogeneous solution with water. This process is the result of the electrostatic interactions between the water molecules and the ions (sodium cations, Na⁺, and chloride anions, Cl⁻) in the salt crystal. The strong attraction between the water molecules and the ions allows for the dissolution of salt in water, and the resulting solution is stable at normal temperatures.

Similar to NaCl, potassium chloride (KCl) also dissolves in water, forming a solution. The dissolution process is driven by the electrostatic interactions between the ions in KCl (potassium cations, K⁺, and chloride anions, Cl⁻) and water molecules. This results in a homogeneous mixture, which remains stable at normal temperatures.
All above explanations.

questions.text: The correct order of the strength of H-bonds is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: H-F (Hydrogen Fluoride): Hydrogen fluoride forms the strongest hydrogen bonds among the compounds listed. This is primarily because fluorine (F) is highly electronegative, which means it strongly attracts the electrons in the H-F bond. The high electronegativity of fluorine results in a significant partial negative charge on the fluoride ion (F⁻) and a significant partial positive charge on the hydrogen atom (H⁺). This strong charge separation leads to strong electrostatic interactions, resulting in powerful hydrogen bonding.H-O (Water): Water also forms relatively strong hydrogen bonds. Oxygen (O) is electronegative, but not as electronegative as fluorine. The O-H bond in water exhibits some charge separation, and this results in moderate hydrogen bonding.H-N (Ammonia): Ammonia forms weaker hydrogen bonds compared to water and hydrogen fluoride. Nitrogen (N) is less electronegative than oxygen, so the N-H bond in ammonia has less charge separation, leading to weaker hydrogen bonding.In general, the strength of hydrogen bonding is influenced by the electronegativity of the atom bonded to hydrogen and the size of the atom. Electronegative atoms that can readily accept hydrogen bonds tend to create stronger hydrogen bonds. Smaller atoms with more localized electron density also create stronger hydrogen bonds.So, the trend can be explained by the electronegativity order (F > O > N) and the resulting charge separation in the H-X bonds, where X represents the electronegative atom. This order corresponds to the strength of hydrogen bonding in these compounds.

H-N (Hydrogen Nitride): The covalent bond between hydrogen and nitrogen (H-N) is relatively strong compared to H-O and H-F. Nitrogen is less electronegative than oxygen and fluorine, resulting in a stronger H-N covalent bond. This greater covalent bond strength is primarily due to the differences in electronegativity.H-O (Hydrogen Oxide): The covalent bond between hydrogen and oxygen (H-O) is weaker than H-N due to oxygen's higher electronegativity. Oxygen attracts electrons more strongly, leading to a weaker H-O covalent bond.H-F (Hydrogen Fluoride): The covalent bond between hydrogen and fluorine (H-F) is even weaker than H-O due to the exceptionally high electronegativity of fluorine. Fluorine's strong electron-attracting ability results in a weaker H-F covalent bond.So, in this context, we are indeed describing the strength of the covalent bonds between hydrogen and these elements, and the trend you've provided aligns with the electronegativity order (F > O > N). This order corresponds to the strength of the covalent bonds between hydrogen and the respective atom.

H-O (Hydrogen Oxide - Water): The covalent bond between hydrogen and oxygen (H-O) is the strongest among the three. This is primarily because oxygen is more electronegative than nitrogen or fluorine. The higher electronegativity of oxygen results in a stronger H-O covalent bond. In water (H2O), the O-H covalent bonds are relatively strong.H-N (Hydrogen Nitrogen - Ammonia): The covalent bond between hydrogen and nitrogen (H-N) is weaker than H-O due to the lower electronegativity of nitrogen compared to oxygen. Nitrogen attracts electrons less strongly, leading to a weaker H-N covalent bond. In ammonia (NH3), the N-H covalent bonds are less strong than O-H bonds in water.H-F (Hydrogen Fluoride): The covalent bond between hydrogen and fluorine (H-F) is the weakest among the three. This is because fluorine is the most electronegative element, and it attracts electrons very strongly, resulting in a weaker H-F covalent bond. In hydrogen fluoride (HF), the F-H covalent bonds are relatively weak.So, the trend reflects the strength of the covalent bonds between hydrogen and these elements and aligns with the electronegativity order (O > N > F). In this context, O-H bonds are the strongest, N-H bonds are weaker, and F-H bonds are the weakest.

H-F (Hydrogen Fluoride): The covalent bond between hydrogen and fluorine (H-F) is the strongest among the three. This is primarily due to the high electronegativity of fluorine. Fluorine is the most electronegative element in the periodic table, meaning it attracts electrons very strongly, resulting in a strong H-F covalent bond.H-N (Hydrogen Nitrogen): The covalent bond between hydrogen and nitrogen (H-N) is weaker than H-F but stronger than H-O. Nitrogen is less electronegative than fluorine, so the H-N bond is weaker compared to H-F, but it's stronger than the H-O bond.H-O (Hydrogen Oxygen): The covalent bond between hydrogen and oxygen (H-O) is the weakest among the three. Oxygen is less electronegative than fluorine, and therefore the H-O bond is the least strong.In this context, the trend you've provided accurately reflects the relative strengths of the covalent bonds between hydrogen and these elements, following the electronegativity order (F > N > O). The higher the electronegativity of the atom bonded to hydrogen, the stronger the covalent bond is.

questions.text: Phosphorous hydride’s bond angle is closer to 90° while the bond angle of ammonium hydride is closer to 104.5°. Among the following, which one explains this structural feature correctly?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Nitrogen bond pair electron cloud concentrates near the central atom because of its higher electronegativity, thus the bond pair – bond pair repulsion increases which in turn decreases the bond angle in NH3.
options.explanation: In ammonia (NH3), nitrogen (N) forms three sigma (σ) bonds with three hydrogen (H) atoms. These bonds are formed when electrons are shared between the nitrogen and hydrogen atoms, creating bond pairs. The presence of these bond pairs leads to electron density around the central nitrogen atom.Nitrogen is more electronegative than hydrogen, which means it attracts electrons more strongly. As a result, the electron cloud associated with the bond pairs is more concentrated around the nitrogen atom. This increased electron density around the nitrogen atom leads to greater electron-electron repulsion between the bond pairs.According to VSEPR (Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion) theory, electrons in the bond pairs and lone pairs around a central atom repel each other. These repulsions influence the geometry of the molecule and the bond angles. In the case of ammonia, the increased electron density around the central nitrogen atom results in stronger bond pair – bond pair repulsions.Stronger bond pair – bond pair repulsions push the hydrogen atoms away from each other. As a result, the bond angles in ammonia (the angle between the hydrogen-nitrogen-hydrogen atoms) are reduced from what they would be in the absence of this repulsion.The bond angles in ammonia are approximately 107 degrees, which is less than the ideal tetrahedral angle of 109.5 degrees because of the increased electron density around the central nitrogen atom, causing greater repulsion between the bond pairs. This results in a bent or "pyramidal" molecular geometry.In summary, the higher electronegativity of nitrogen causes the bond pair electron cloud to be concentrated around the central atom, leading to increased bond pair – bond pair repulsion and a decrease in the bond angle in NH3

options.text: Due to larger size of the lone pair electron cloud, there is larger lone pair – bond pair repulsion in PH3 compared to NH3.
options.explanation: In PH3, the lone pair of electrons on phosphorus occupies a larger volume due to phosphorus's larger size compared to nitrogen. This larger lone pair creates stronger lone pair – bond pair repulsions, pushing the bonded hydrogen atoms closer together. As a result, the bond angle in PH3 is larger than that in NH3, approximately around 93.5 degrees for PH3 compared to about 107 degrees for NH3.

options.text: Due to quite high energy difference between 3s and 3p orbitals, the lone pair on phosphorous prefers to occupy unhybridized s-orbital rather than hybridized sp3 hybridized orbital which causes its s-orbital energy to increase.
options.explanation: The high energy difference between the 3s and 3p orbitals in phosphorous results in the lone pair electrons preferring to occupy the unhybridized 3s orbital. This preference occurs because the energy required to promote electrons to the higher-energy 3p orbital is significant, causing the 3s orbital to have a lower energy level. Consequently, the s-orbital energy increases as it becomes populated by the lone pair, leading to a greater difference in energy between the s and p orbitals in phosphorus

options.text: Phosphorous forms pπ - dπ bonds while nitrogen doesn’t.
options.explanation: Phosphorus has empty d orbitals available in its valence shell, which allows it to form pπ - dπ bonds. In contrast, nitrogen lacks d orbitals in its valence shell, so it cannot form such bonds. This difference in orbital availability leads to distinct bonding behavior between phosphorus and nitrogen.

questions.explanation: The energy difference between 3s and 3p orbitals is quite high in group 15 and group 16 hydrides (except for NH3 and H2O). The orbital energy of 3s increases so much by hybridization so lone pair tends to occupy an unhybridized s orbital. . For example in PH3, 600 kJ mol-1 of energy is required to hybridize the central atom. In order to avoid such energy demanding hybridization, by leaving the lone pair in the spherical s orbital, P forms bonds with unhybridized p orbitals. Thus, the bond angle in PH3 is closer to 90° while that in NH3 is 104.5°.

questions.text: Decreasing order of size of various hybrid orbitals is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: the decreasing degree of p-character in hybrid orbitals. sp hybridization has the highest p-character, leading to a linear geometry. sp² hybridization has intermediate p-character, resulting in a trigonal planar geometry. sp³ hybridization has the lowest p-character, leading to a tetrahedral geometry. This variation in p-character affects bond angles and molecular shapes.

The order "sp³ > sp² > sp" represents the decreasing degree of p-character in hybrid orbitals. In sp³ hybridization, there is a higher proportion of p-character, leading to a tetrahedral geometry with 109.5° bond angles. In sp² hybridization, the p-character decreases, resulting in a trigonal planar geometry with 120° bond angles. In sp hybridization, the p-character is the lowest, creating a linear geometry with 180° bond angles.

In the order "sp² > sp > sp³," the decreasing degree of p-character in hybrid orbitals results in different bond angles and molecular geometries. sp² hybridization has moderate p-character, leading to a trigonal planar geometry, while sp hybridization has higher p-character, resulting in a linear geometry. sp³ hybridization has the lowest p-character, leading to a tetrahedral geometry. These hybridizations play a crucial role in determining the shape and properties of molecules

The order "sp > sp³ > sp²" corresponds to the decreasing degree of p-character in hybrid orbitals. In sp hybridization, there is a higher proportion of p-character, leading to a linear geometry. In contrast, sp³ hybridization has more s-character, resulting in a tetrahedral geometry, and sp² hybridization has intermediate p-character, leading to a trigonal planar geometry. This trend in hybridization affects the geometry and properties of molecules

questions.explanation: The order "sp³ > sp² > sp" represents the decreasing degree of p-character in hybrid orbitals. In sp³ hybridization, there is a higher proportion of p-character, leading to a tetrahedral geometry with 109.5° bond angles. In sp² hybridization, the p-character decreases, resulting in a trigonal planar geometry with 120° bond angles. In sp hybridization, the p-character is the lowest, creating a linear geometry with 180° bond angles.

questions.text: Maleic acid is stronger than fumaric acid because :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fumaric acid exhibits intermolecular hydrogen bonding due to the presence of hydrogen bond donor (the hydroxyl group, -OH) and acceptor (the carbonyl group, C=O) sites in its molecular structure. Hydrogen bonds form between the oxygen atom of the hydroxyl group and the hydrogen atom of the carbonyl group in adjacent molecules, leading to the association of fumaric acid molecules through these interactions, which enhances its physical properties and stability.

Fumaric acid exhibits intermolecular hydrogen bonding due to the presence of hydrogen bond donor (the hydroxyl group, -OH) and acceptor (the carbonyl group, C=O) sites in its molecular structure. Hydrogen bonds form between the oxygen atom of the hydroxyl group and the hydrogen atom of the carbonyl group in adjacent molecules, leading to the association of fumaric acid molecules through these interactions, which enhances its physical properties and stability.

Maleic acid is a dibasic acid because it contains two carboxylic acid (-COOH) functional groups in its molecular structure. Each carboxylic acid group can donate a proton (H+) in an acid-base reaction, making maleic acid capable of releasing two protons, or hydrogen ions, in solution. This property gives it the ability to act as a strong acid and form salts with bases, making it a diprotic or dibasic acid.

options.text: Maleic acid shows chelation or intramolecular H-bonding
options.explanation: Maleic acid is a dibasic acid because it contains two carboxylic acid (-COOH) functional groups in its molecular structure. Each carboxylic acid group can donate a proton (H+) in an acid-base reaction, making maleic acid capable of releasing two protons, or hydrogen ions, in solution. This property gives it the ability to act as a strong acid and form salts with bases, making it a diprotic or dibasic acid.

questions.explanation: Maleic acid shows intramolecular H-bonding (chelation) and thus–COOH is stabilized. Maleic acid, being a cis-isomer is capable of forming intra-hydrogen bond after losing H+ ion, making the conjugate base much stable than the trans form (fumaric acid) because both the oxygen atoms have strong interaction with the remaining H-atom.

questions.text: Among LiCl, BeCl2, BCl3 and CCl4 the covalent bond character follows the order:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The given sequence, LiCl > BeCl2 > BCl3 > CCl4, represents the increasing ionic character and boiling points of these compounds.LiCl is primarily ionic and has the highest ionic character, leading to a relatively high boiling point.BeCl2 has a significant covalent character but still exhibits some ionic character due to the electronegativity difference between beryllium (Be) and chlorine (Cl).BCl3 is a polar covalent compound, and its boiling point is lower than BeCl2 and LiCl due to the increased covalent character.CCl4 is a nonpolar covalent compound with the least ionic character, and it has the lowest boiling point among the compounds listed because it is dominated by covalent forces.So, the trend in boiling points follows the order of increasing ionic character and decreasing covalent character, leading to higher boiling points from LiCl to CCl4.
The covalent character of ionic compound can be determined by Fajans rules,the cation with higher positive charge has greater covalent character than cation with lower positive charge.

The given sequence, LiCl > BeCl2 > CCl4 > BCl3, represents the increasing boiling points of these compounds.LiCl has a relatively high boiling point due to the ionic nature of the Li-Cl bond.BeCl2 has a higher boiling point than LiCl because it is a covalent compound, and the larger number of electrons leads to stronger London dispersion forces.CCl4, a nonpolar covalent compound with a large number of electrons, has a higher boiling point due to significant London dispersion forces.BCl3, despite its polar covalent nature, has the lowest boiling point among the listed compounds because it lacks the large electron cloud and symmetry seen in CCl4, resulting in weaker dispersion forces.

The given sequence, LiCl < BeCl3 < BCl3 > CCl4, represents the increasing size and polarizability of the central atom in these compounds.LiCl has a small lithium (Li) atom, which results in limited polarizability.BeCl3 has a beryllium (Be) atom, which is slightly larger and more polarizable than Li but smaller than the following compounds.BCl3 features boron (B), which is larger and more polarizable than both Li and Be..CCl4 has a larger central carbon (C) atom, leading to increased polarizability.The trend in this sequence is influenced by the size of the central atom, impacting the strength of the dispersion forces between molecules, with CCl4 having the largest central atom and the strongest dispersion forces. As a result, the boiling points and intermolecular forces follow a similar trend with LiCl < BeCl3 < BCl3 < CCl4.

questions.explanation: Smaller cation causes more polarization of anion. The covalent character of ionic compound can be determined by Fajans rules,the cation with higher positive charge has greater covalent character than cation with lower positive charge

questions.text: PCl5 exists but NCl5 does not because :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: PCl5 forms five bonds by using the d-orbitals to "expand the octet" and have more "places" to put bonding pairs of electrons. NCl5 does not exist because there are no d-orbitals in the second energy level. Therefore there is no way to arrange five pairs of bonding electrons around a nitrogen atom.

Nitrogen pentachloride (NCl5) is indeed an unstable compound under normal conditions. It is a highly reactive and explosive compound. NCl5 is not commonly encountered or studied due to its instability and tendency to decompose explosively into nitrogen trichloride (NCl3) and chlorine gas (Cl2).

options.text: nitrogen atom is much smaller than P
options.explanation: A nitrogen (N) atom is significantly smaller than a phosphorus (P) atom. This difference in atomic size is due to the number of protons, neutrons, and electrons in each atom. Nitrogen, with its smaller number of electrons and protons, has a smaller atomic radius compared to phosphorus, which has a greater number of electrons and protons, leading to a larger atomic radius.

Nitrogen (N2) in its molecular form is often considered inert under typical conditions. This is because the nitrogen-nitrogen (N≡N) triple bond is very strong and stable, making it less reactive than many other elements or compounds. Nitrogen gas, which makes up the majority of Earth's atmosphere, does not readily react with other substances at room temperature and pressure.However, it's essential to note that nitrogen can be made to react under specific conditions or with the introduction of energy or catalysts. For example, in industrial processes, nitrogen gas can be fixed to form ammonia (NH3) through the Haber-Bosch process, which is vital for fertilizer production. Additionally, nitrogen compounds can be reactive in various chemical reactions.

questions.text: The maximum covalency is equal to :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The number of unpaired s-and p-electrons
The actual number of s-and p-electrons in the outermost shell.

questions.explanation: In chemical bonding, atoms can share electrons to form covalent bonds. The number of covalent bonds an atom can form, known as its covalency, depends on the number of available valence electrons. Valence electrons are the electrons in the outermost energy level or shell of an atom.For elements in the s and p blocks of the periodic table (groups 1-7 and 13-18), the valence electrons are found in the s and p orbitals, respectively. The s orbital can hold a maximum of 2 electrons, and each p orbital can hold a maximum of 6 electrons.To determine the number of valence electrons for an atom, you can look at its position in the periodic table. The group number tells you the number of valence electrons for elements in groups 1-18. For example, elements in group 1 have 1 valence electron, elements in group 14 have 4 valence electrons, and elements in group 17 have 7 valence electrons.The maximum covalency of an atom is related to its valence electrons because covalent bonds are formed by sharing these electrons. The general rule is that the maximum covalency can be determined by dividing the number of valence electrons by 2. This is because each covalent bond involves the sharing of two electrons—one from each atom.

options.explanation: The ionic size of the fluoride ion (F⁻) is relatively small. This is because the fluoride ion has gained an extra electron to achieve a stable, full electron configuration, which results in an increase in the electron cloud's negative charge. The additional electron leads to increased electron-electron repulsions, causing the electron cloud to expand slightly.

The ionic size of the oxide ion (O²⁻) is relatively large. This is because the oxide ion has gained two extra electrons to achieve a stable, full electron configuration, which significantly increases the negative charge in the electron cloud. The addition of these extra electrons results in significant electron-electron repulsions, causing the electron cloud to expand, and the ion to become relatively large compared to the parent oxygen atom.
Ionic size decreases along the period form left to right. More over anions are of larger size as compare to cations. So, order of ionic radius is N-3>O-2> F-1> Na+1.

The ionic size of the sodium ion (Na⁺) is relatively small. When sodium (Na) loses an electron to form the Na⁺ ion, it has fewer electrons than in its neutral state. This results in a decreased electron cloud and a smaller ionic size. The loss of an electron creates a positively charged ion with a reduced electron cloud, making the Na⁺ ion smaller than the neutral sodium atom.

options.explanation: On the periodic table, atomic radius generally decreases as you move from left to right across a period (due to increasing nuclear charge) and increases as you move down a group (due to the increasing number of electron shells.

"A⁺" refers to a cation (positively charged ion). Cations are generally smaller than their parent neutral atoms (A) because they lose electrons, leading to a reduced electron cloud and a smaller ionic size."A" is the neutral atom with an equal number of protons and electrons, and its size is between the sizes of the corresponding cation.

"A⁺" refers to a cation (positively charged ion). Cations are generally smaller than their parent neutral atoms (A) because they lose electrons, leading to a reduced electron cloud and a smaller ionic size."A" is the neutral atom with an equal number of protons and electrons, and its size is between the sizes of the corresponding cation (A⁺) and anion (A⁻)."A⁻" represents an anion (negatively charged ion). Anions are typically larger than their parent neutral atoms (A) due to the addition of extra electrons, which increases the electron cloud's size.So, the trend is that cations are smaller than neutral atoms, which, in turn, are smaller than anions.

A⁻" refers to an anion (negatively charged ion). Anions are typically larger than their parent neutral atoms (A) due to the addition of extra electrons, which increases the electron cloud's size.A⁺" represents a cation (positively charged ion). Cations are generally smaller than their parent neutral atoms because they lose electrons, leading to a reduced electron cloud and a smaller ionic size."A" is the neutral atom with an equal number of protons and electrons, and its size is between the sizes of the corresponding anion (A⁻) and cation (A⁺).So, the trend is that anions are larger than neutral atoms, which, in turn, are larger than cations.

questions.text: The following molecules has linear structure except?

options.explanation: The geometry of CS2 is linear. This is because the central carbon (C) atom has two double bonds with the two sulfur (S) atoms. Since there are no lone pairs of electrons on the central carbon atom, the molecule has a linear shape with a bond angle of 180 degrees.

The geometry of CO2 is linear. This is because the central carbon (C) atom has two double bonds with the two oxygen (O) atoms and there are no lone pairs of electrons on the central carbon atom, hence the molecule has a linear shape with a bond angle of 180 degrees.

SO2 is a bent or V-shaped molecule. It has a bond angle of approximately 120 degrees between the sulfur atom (S) and the two oxygen atoms (O), resulting in a bent molecular geometry. This bent shape is due to the presence of two lone pairs of electrons on the sulfur atom, which cause repulsion and push the oxygen atoms closer together.

The geometry of BeCl2 is linear. This is because Beryllium (Be) has two valence electrons, and it forms two covalent bonds with chlorine (Cl) atoms. Since there are no lone pairs of electrons on the central beryllium atom, the molecule has a linear shape with a bond angle of 180 degrees.

questions.explanation: SO2 is a bent or V-shaped molecule. It has a bond angle of approximately 120 degrees between the sulfur atom (S) and the two oxygen atoms (O), resulting in a bent molecular geometry. This bent shape is due to the presence of two lone pairs of electrons on the sulfur atom, which cause repulsion and push the oxygen atoms closer together.

questions.text: In which of following species central atom is not sp3 hybridized ?

options.explanation: In the case of NH4+ (ammonium ion), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp3. It forms four sigma bonds with four hydrogen atoms, and the central nitrogen atom undergoes hybridization to accommodate these bonds. This results in the formation of four equivalent sigma bonds and a tetrahedral molecular geometry around the nitrogen atom.

The NH2- ion has a bent (angular) shape with bond angles of approximately 104.5 degrees due to repulsion between lone pairs and bonding pairs. It has sp3 hybridization and two lone pairs ,hence this is an incorrect option

In NH3 (ammonia), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp3. The nitrogen atom forms three sigma bonds with three hydrogen atoms, and there is one lone pair of electrons on nitrogen. This results in a tetrahedral molecular geometry around the nitrogen atom, which corresponds to sp3 hybridization.

In NO3- (Nitrate ion), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp2. The nitrogen atom forms one sigma bond with one of the oxygen atoms and two pi bonds with the other two oxygen atoms, resulting in a total of three bonds. This corresponds to sp2 hybridization, and the nitrogen atom has one lone pair of electrons. The molecule has a trigonal planar molecular geometry around the central nitrogen atom ,hence this is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: In NO3- (Nitrate ion), the central nitrogen atom is in a hybridization state of sp2. The nitrogen atom forms one sigma bond with one of the oxygen atoms and two pi bonds with the other two oxygen atoms, resulting in a total of three bonds. This corresponds to sp2 hybridization, and the nitrogen atom has one lone pair of electrons. The molecule has a trigonal planar molecular geometry around the central nitrogen atom.

questions.text: Choose the incorrect relationship of bond energies :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The bond energy of Br2 (diatomic bromine molecule) is generally greater than that of I2 (diatomic iodine molecule).This is because the bond energy, which represents the energy required to break the bond in a diatomic molecule, generally increases as you move across a period in the periodic table from left to right ,hence this is an incorrect option.

The bond energy of Cl2 (diatomic chlorine molecule) is generally lower than that of F2 (diatomic fluorine molecule). This is because, as you move from left to right across a period in the periodic table, the atomic radius decreases, and the strength of covalent bonds in diatomic molecules tends to increase.As a result, the F2 bond is generally stronger and has a higher bond energy than the Cl2 bond. Breaking the F2 bond requires more energy than breaking the Cl2 bond ,hence this is the correct option.

Carbon-carbon triple bonds are typically stronger and have higher bond energies than nitrogen-nitrogen triple bonds because carbon atoms are larger in size than nitrogen atoms. In a C≡C bond, the carbon atoms are held together by strong sigma (σ) and pi (π) bonds, resulting in a higher bond energy ,hence this is an incorrect option.
In the light of the discussions above ,this option is incorrect.

Breaking the F2 bond requires more energy than breaking the Cl2 bond.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has non-zero dipole moment ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CH3CI is a polar molecule and has non-zero dipole moment due to polarity between C and Cl ,hence this is the correct option.
The net dipole moment of the BF3 molecule is zero, and it is non-polar ,hence this is an incorrect option.
The dipole moment of CO2 (carbon dioxide) is zero because of its linear molecular geometry and the symmetry of its polar bonds ,hence this is an incorrect option.

The dipole moment of CCl4 is zero because its symmetrical tetrahedral molecular geometry that causes the polar C-Cl bond dipoles to cancel each other out, resulting in a nonpolar molecule ,hence this is an incorrect option.

questions.text: Maximum dipole moment is possessed by ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Water is a highly polar molecule due to its bent molecular geometry and the electronegativity difference between hydrogen and oxygen atoms, resulting in the highest dipole moment among all the given options ,hence it is the correct answer.H2O (μ= 1.85D)

H2S (hydrogen sulfide) also has a dipole moment, but it is less than that of H2O. The bent molecular geometry and the electronegativity difference between sulfur and hydrogen result in polarity, but it is weaker than in H2O.H2O (μ= 1.85D),CO (p=0.12D),H2S (μ=0.95D),CH4 (μ= 0.0D) ,this shows that it is an incorrect option.

CO (carbon monoxide) has a dipole moment because of the polar C-O bond, but it is less than H2O. CO is a linear molecule, and the difference in electronegativity between carbon and oxygen contributes to its dipole moment.H2O (μ= 1.85D),CO (p=0.12D),H2S (μ=0.95D),CH4 (μ= 0.0D) ,this shows that it is an incorrect option.

CH4 (methane) has a tetrahedral geometry, but it is a nonpolar molecule because the four identical C-H bonds are symmetrically arranged, canceling out any dipole moment.H2O (μ= 1.85D),CO (μ=0.12D),H2S (μ=0.95D),CH4 (μ= 0.0D) ,this shows that it is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: H2O (μ= 1.85D),CO (p=0.12D),H2S (μ=0.95D),CH4 (μ= 0.0D).Water is a highly polar molecule due to its bent molecular geometry and the electronegativity difference between hydrogen and oxygen atoms.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules does not have planar geometry?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The carbon atoms in benzene form a hexagonal ring, and the carbon-carbon bonds are in the same plane ,hence it has a planar geometry and this option is incorrect.

In a BF3 molecule, boron forms three sigma bonds with three fluorine atoms. The arrangement of these bonds is such that the three fluorine atoms are located at the corners of a trigonal planar geometry around the central boron atom ,hence this is an incorrect option.

In an ethene molecule, the two carbon atoms are bonded to each other by a carbon-carbon double bond (a sigma bond and a pi bond), and each carbon atom is also bonded to one hydrogen atom. The molecule has a planar, flat structure, with the carbon-carbon double bond and the two carbon-hydrogen single bonds all lying in the same plane ,hence this is an incorrect option.

Phosphorus trichloride, PCl3, does not have a planar geometry. It has a trigonal pyramidal molecular geometry. In a PCl3 molecule, phosphorus forms three sigma bonds with three chlorine atoms.The arrangement of these bonds is such that the three chlorine atoms are located at the corners of a trigonal pyramid around the central phosphorus atom ,hence this is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Phosphorus trichloride does not have a planar geometry. It has a trigonal pyramidal molecular geometry. In a PCl3 molecule, phosphorus forms three sigma bonds with three chlorine atoms.The arrangement of these bonds is such that the three chlorine atoms are located at the corners of a trigonal pyramid around the central phosphorus atom.

questions.text: Which one of the following pair of compounds contains one that is giant ionic and one that is simple molecule?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Al2O3 (aluminum oxide) is a giant ionic compound. It consists of a three-dimensional lattice of aluminum ions (Al+3) and oxygen ions (O-2), held together by ionic bonds. It forms a crystal lattice structure.Al2Cl6 (aluminum hexachloride) is a molecular compound and is made up of covalently bonded aluminum and chlorine atoms. It is not a giant ionic compound like Al2O3,hence this is the correct option.

P4O10 (phosphorus pentoxide) is a covalent compound composed of a network of covalently bonded phosphorus and oxygen atoms, forming a giant covalent structure.PCl3 (phosphorus trichloride) is a molecular compound where phosphorus is covalently bonded to three chlorine atoms, and the molecules are held together by covalent bonds. It does not have a giant ionic structure ,hence this option is incorrect.

SiO2 and SiCl4 are not a pair of compounds with one being a giant ionic compound and one being a simple molecular compound. Both of these compounds have a covalent molecular structure ,hence this option is incorrect.

HF is a covalent compound.PbCl4 (lead(IV) chloride) is a molecular compound consisting of lead and chlorine atoms, and the molecules are held together by covalent bonds. It is not a giant ionic compound.None of the compounds in this pair fits the criteria of one being a giant ionic compound and the other being a simple molecule ,hence this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Al2O3 (aluminum oxide) is a giant ionic compound. It consists of a three-dimensional lattice of aluminum ions (Al+3) and oxygen ions (O-2), held together by ionic bonds. It forms a crystal lattice structure.Al2Cl6 (aluminum hexachloride) is a molecular compound and is made up of covalently bonded aluminum and chlorine atoms. It is not a giant ionic compound like Al2O3.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has only non-coplanar tetrahedral geometry?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: NF3 (nitrogen trifluoride) does not have a tetrahedral geometry, nor is it a planar molecule. It has a trigonal pyramidal molecular geometry ,hence this is an incorrect option.

The molecule C3H8, which is propane, has only non-coplanar tetrahedral geometry.Propane (C3H8) consists of three carbon atoms bonded together in a straight chain, with each carbon atom forming single bonds with three hydrogen atoms. The carbon-carbon bonds in propane are sigma (σ) bonds, which allow free rotation around the carbon-carbon axis. As a result, the molecule is not planar (coplanar) but adopts a three-dimensional tetrahedral geometry around each carbon atom ,hence this is the correct answer.

C2Cl4 (tetrachloroethylene) does not have a tetrahedral geometry; it has a non-planar tetrahedral geometry. In C2Cl4, the two carbon atoms are bonded to each other by a carbon-carbon double bond, and each carbon atom is bonded to two chlorine atoms. This results in a tetrahedral geometry around each carbon atom, with the two chlorine atoms and the carbon-carbon double bond forming a tetrahedral shape ,hence this is an incorrect option.

C3H6 (propene) has a non-planar tetrahedral geometry around the carbon atoms. The molecule consists of three carbon atoms and six hydrogen atoms, and the central carbon atom forms a double bond with one of the adjacent carbon atoms. The arrangement of the three carbon atoms and their associated bonds results in a non-planar tetrahedral geometry around each carbon atom ,hence this is an incorrrect option.

questions.explanation: The molecule C3H8, which is propane, has only non-coplanar tetrahedral geometry.Propane (C3H8) consists of three carbon atoms bonded together in a straight chain, with each carbon atom forming single bonds with three hydrogen atoms. The carbon-carbon bonds in propane are sigma (σ) bonds, which allow free rotation around the carbon-carbon axis. As a result, the molecule is not planar (coplanar) but adopts a three-dimensional tetrahedral geometry around each carbon atom ,hence this is the correct answer.

questions.text: Which property is not shown by covalent compounds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: They are present in the gas, liquid or solid state
options.explanation: Covalent compounds can be present in all three states of matter (gas, liquid, and solid) depending on the specific compound, its physical properties, and the temperature and pressure conditions under which it is found ,hence this is not the correct answer.

options.text: They are more reactive than ionic compounds
options.explanation: The ionic compounds exist in the form of ions in an aqueous solution. The chemical reaction between ions occur rapidly.Since, there is no strong electrical force to speed up a chemical reaction (like in ionic reaction),the covalent bonds are generally much slower to react as they involve bond breaking and making of bonds.Covalent bonds react in a variety of ways and their reactivity depends upon the way a reaction proceeds and the kind of a reaction ,hence this is the correct answer.
Covalent compounds can exhibit both isomerism and resonance ,hence this option is incorrect.

options.text: They have low melting points and boiling points as compared to ionic compounds
options.explanation: Covalent compounds generally have lower melting points and boiling points as compared to ionic compounds ,hence this is an incorrect option

The chemical reaction between ions occur rapidly.Since, there is no strong electrical force to speed up a chemical reaction (like in ionic reaction),the covalent bonds are generally much slower to react as they involve bond breaking and making of bonds.Covalent bonds react in a variety of ways and their reactivity depends upon the way a reaction proceeds and the kind of a reaction ,so the statement given in option 'B' is not true about covelant compounds and ultimately is the correct answer to this question.

questions.text: Which one of the following molecules shows a linear geometry?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The geometry of H2O (water) is bent or V-shaped. Water has a bent molecular geometry because the central oxygen atom is bonded to two hydrogen atoms, resulting in a molecular shape where the two hydrogen atoms are oriented at an angle of approximately 104.5 degrees relative to each other ,hence this is not the correct answer.

The geometry of Cl2O (dichlorine monoxide) is bent or V-shaped. Cl2O has a bent molecular geometry because the central oxygen atom is bonded to two chlorine atoms, resulting in a molecular shape where the two chlorine atoms are oriented at an angle less than 120 degrees relative to each other ,hence this is not the correct answer.

The geometry of HCN (hydrogen cyanide) is linear. In HCN, the central carbon atom is bonded to a hydrogen atom and a nitrogen atom. The linear molecular geometry arises from the arrangement of the atoms, with the hydrogen and nitrogen atoms on opposite sides of the carbon atom, forming a straight line. This linear geometry results from the arrangement of the bonding and lone pairs of electrons around the carbon atom, leading to a linear shape with a bond angle of approximately 180 degrees ,hence this is the correct answer.

In C2H4, the two carbon atoms are bonded together by a carbon-carbon double bond (consisting of a sigma bond and a pi bond), and each carbon atom is also bonded to a hydrogen atom. The arrangement of atoms and bonds results in a planar, flat structure with the carbon-carbon double bond and the two carbon-hydrogen single bonds all lying in the same plane ,hence this is not the correct answer.

questions.explanation: The geometry of HCN (hydrogen cyanide) is linear. In HCN, the central carbon atom is bonded to a hydrogen atom and a nitrogen atom. The linear molecular geometry arises from the arrangement of the atoms, with the hydrogen and nitrogen atoms on opposite sides of the carbon atom, forming a straight line. This linear geometry results from the arrangement of the bonding and lone pairs of electrons around the carbon atom, leading to a linear shape with a bond angle of approximately 180 degrees.

questions.text: Second and higher electron affinity values are positive because of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Repulsion between electrons and negatively charged ions
options.explanation: Second and higher electron affinity values are positive because adding an additional electron to an already negatively charged ion (anion) is energetically unfavorable.When you try to add another electron to an already negatively charged ion, it becomes increasingly difficult. The additional electron experiences a strong electrostatic repulsion from the negatively charged ion, making the process energetically unfavorable ,hence this is the correct answer.

options.text: Attraction between electrons and positively charged ions
options.explanation: Second and higher electron affinity values are positive because adding an additional electron to an already negatively charged ion (anion) is energetically unfavorable.When you try to add another electron to an already negatively charged ion, it becomes increasingly difficult. The additional electron experiences a strong electrostatic repulsion from the negatively charged ion, making the process energetically unfavorable ,hence this is an incorrect option.

options.text: First repulsion between electrons and then attraction
options.explanation: Second and higher electron affinity values are positive because adding an additional electron to an already negatively charged ion (anion) is energetically unfavorable.When you try to add another electron to an already negatively charged ion, it becomes increasingly difficult. The additional electron experiences a strong electrostatic repulsion from the negatively charged ion, making the process energetically unfavorable ,hence this is an incorrect option.
Only A is correct as explained above.

questions.text: In which of the following ionic bond is formed between atoms of elements?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Aluminum (Al) is a metal, and chlorine (Cl) is a non-metal. The electronegativity difference between Al and Cl is around 1.5, which is significant but not high enough for the formation of a pure ionic bond. In AlCl3, aluminum donates electrons to chlorine, creating Al+3and Cl-ions. While there's some ionic character, the bond is primarily covalent due to the sharing of electrons.Since ,it is partially ionic and mostly covelant hence this is not the correct answer.

Hydrogen (H) is a non-metal, and chlorine (Cl) is also a non-metal. The electronegativity difference between H and Cl is about 0.9, indicating a polar covalent bond. In HCl, the electron is shared but not equally, resulting in partial charges on the atoms.Since it has a covelant bond so this option is incorrect.

Boron (B) is a metalloid, and fluorine (F) is a nonmetal. Boron trifluoride (BF3) involves covalent bonds between boron and fluorine atoms. Boron shares its electrons with fluorine, and it does not strictly satisfy the octet rule, making it a covalent compound ,hence this option is incorrect.

Sodium (Na) is a metal, and hydrogen (H) is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference between Na and H is substantial. Sodium loses an electron to become Na+, and hydrogen gains an electron to become H-. The strong electrostatic attraction between the oppositely charged ions forms an ionic bond in compounds like sodium hydride (NaH) ,hence this is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) is a metal, and hydrogen (H) is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference between Na and H is substantial. Sodium loses an electron to become Na+, and hydrogen gains an electron to become H-. The strong electrostatic attraction between the oppositely charged ions forms an ionic bond in compounds like sodium hydride (NaH).

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has six bonding electrons?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In ethene molecule there are 12 bonding electrons : Carbon-carbon double bond: 2 bonding electrons for the sigma bond and 2 bonding electrons for the pi bond, total= 4 bonding electrons. Carbon-hydrogen sigma bonds: There are 4 hydrogen atoms bonded to the two carbon atoms, and each carbon-hydrogen bond involves 2 electrons (a sigma bond). So, there are a total of 8 bonding electrons from carbon-hydrogen sigma bonds. Adding these together, you have a total of 4 + 8 = 12 bonding electrons in ethene.

There should be four bonding electrons in hydrogen sulfide (H2S). Each covalent bond in H2S involves two electrons, one from each atom. Therefore, the two hydrogen-sulfur bonds in H2S contribute a total of four bonding electrons.
In CO2 ,C atom forms two double bonds with 2 oxygen atoms.One double bond contributes four bonding electrons and 2 will contribute to 8 ,hence CO2 has 8 bonding electrons.

Correct In the nitrogen trichloride molecule (NCl3), there are three single bonds between the nitrogen and chlorine atoms. Each single bond contributes two bonding electrons, and there are three such bonds in nitrogen trichloride ,hence total Bonding Electrons= 3 bonds × 2 electrons/bond = 6 bonding electrons

questions.explanation: In the nitrogen trichloride molecule (NCl3), there are three single bonds between the nitrogen and chlorine atoms. Each single bond contributes two bonding electrons, and there are three such bonds in nitrogen trichloride ,hence total Bonding Electrons= 3 bonds × 2 electrons/bond = 6 bonding electrons

questions.text: If two atoms are bonded in such a way that One member of the covalently bonded molecule donates both electrons that are shared, then what is this type of bond called?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding involves a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly electronegative atom (such as oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom from a neighboring molecule. It's a special type of dipole-dipole interaction, not characterized by one atom donating both electrons in a bond ,hence this is not the correct option.

A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons.In a typical covalent bond, both atoms contribute electron to form the shared pair. The scenario where one atom donates both electrons is specifically referred to as a "coordinate covalent bond." Hence this option is incorrect.

This is the correct term for the type of bond described in the question. A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond or a Lewis acid-base bond, is formed when one atom (the donor) provides both electrons for sharing with another atom (the acceptor). This type of bond is often indicated by an arrow pointing from the donor atom to the acceptor atom.

An electrovalent bond is another term for an ionic bond. Ionic bonds are formed between atoms with significantly different electronegativities, resulting in the transfer of electrons from one atom to another to form positively and negatively charged ions ,hence this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: A coordinate covalent bond, also known as a dative bond or a Lewis acid-base bond, is formed when one atom (the donor) provides both electrons for sharing with another atom (the acceptor). This type of bond is often indicated by an arrow pointing from the donor atom to the acceptor atom.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules shows tetrahedral geometry?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ammonia has a trigonal pyramidal geometry. It consists of three hydrogen atoms and one lone pair of electrons around the central nitrogen atom. This arrangement results in a pyramid-like shape, not a tetrahedron ,hence this option is incorrect.

Sulfur trioxide has a trigonal planar geometry. It consists of three oxygen atoms bonded to the central sulfur atom, arranged in a flat, triangular shape. This geometry is not tetrahedral ,hence this option is incorrect.
The sulfate ion (SO4-2) has a tetrahedral geometry. It consists of four oxygen atoms bonded to the central sulfur atom, forming a tetrahedral arrangement ,hence this is the correct answer.

Sulfur dioxide has a bent or V-shaped geometry. It contains two oxygen atoms bonded to the central sulfur atom, with a lone pair on sulfur causing the molecule to adopt a bent shape. This shape is not tetrahedral ,hence this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The sulfate ion (SO4-2) has a tetrahedral geometry. It consists of four oxygen atoms bonded to the central sulfur atom, forming a tetrahedral arrangement.

questions.text: Which type of bonding is responsible for intermolecular forces in liquid CCI4?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Covalent bonds are intramolecular forces that hold atoms together within a molecule. They are strong forces involving the sharing of electrons. Intermolecular forces, on the other hand, are responsible for interactions between different molecules. Covalent bonding is not directly responsible for the intermolecular forces in liquid CCl4,hence this is not the correct option.

Hydrogen bonding occurs when hydrogen atoms are bonded to highly electronegative atoms (such as nitrogen, oxygen, or fluorine) and interact with other electronegative atoms in neighboring molecules. Carbon tetrachloride (CCl4) does not contain hydrogen atoms directly bonded to electronegative atoms, so hydrogen bonding is not present and this is not the correct option.

The intermolecular forces in liquid CCl4 (carbon tetrachloride) are primarily due to London dispersion forces, also known as van der Waals forces or Instantaneous dipole — induced dipole forces. These forces are relatively weak as compared to covalent or ionic bonds, but they play a significant role in determining the physical properties of molecular compounds like CCl4 ,hence this is the correct answer.

Dipole-dipole forces occur between polar molecules with permanent dipoles. Carbon tetrachloride (CCl4) is a nonpolar molecule with a symmetrical tetrahedral arrangement of chlorine atoms around the central carbon atom. As a result, there is no permanent dipole moment, and dipole-dipole forces are not significant ,hence this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: The intermolecular forces in liquid CCl4 (carbon tetrachloride) are primarily due to London dispersion forces, also known as van der Waals forces or Instantaneous dipole — induced dipole forces. These forces are relatively weak as compared to covalent or ionic bonds, but they play a significant role in determining the physical properties of molecular compounds like CCl4.

questions.text: Which one of the following gaints solids have greater melting and boiling points on the basis of type of bonding?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metallic solids are held together by metallic bonds, which involve a "sea" of delocalized electrons surrounding positively charged metal ions. These electrons contribute to the unique properties of metals, such as electrical conductivity and malleability. While metallic bonds are strong, they are not as strong as the electrostatic forces in ionic solids or the strong covalent bonds in covalent solids. Therefore, metallic solids generally have lower melting and boiling points compared to ionic and covalent solids. Incorrect.

Covalent solids, especially those with a network structure like diamond, are held together by strong covalent bonds between atoms. These bonds are very strong and require a substantial amount of energy to break. Consequently, covalent solids tend to have extremely high melting and boiling points due to the strength of these bonds. An example is diamond, which has a melting point of 3550°C. Correct.

Ionic solids are formed by the attraction between positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions). These electrostatic forces are strong, and ionic solids have high melting and boiling points as a result but not as high as covalent solids. Incorrect.

Molecular solids consist of individual molecules held together by weak intermolecular forces like van der Waals forces or hydrogen bonding. These forces are much weaker than covalent or ionic bonds. As a result, molecular solids generally have lower melting and boiling points. Incorrect.

questions.explanation: covalent solids,especially those with a a network structure like diamond, are held together by strong covalent bonds between atoms. These bonds are very strong and require a substantial amount of energy to break. Consequently, covalent solids tend to have extremely high melting and boiling points due to the strength of these bonds. An example is diamond, which has a melting point of 3550°C.

questions.text: With the increase of which one of the following factors bond energy of a molecule decreases:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The electronegative difference refers to the disparity in electronegativity values between two atoms in a chemical bond. When there's a higher electronegative difference, the bond tends to be more polar, with one atom pulling the shared electrons closer to itself. This polarization results in stronger electrostatic forces between the atoms, enhancing the bond energy. As a result, an increase in electronegative difference leads to an increase, not a decrease, in bond energy. Verdict: Incorrect.

Bond order represents the number of electron pairs shared between two atoms in a bond. A higher bond order implies a stronger bond and typically results in higher bond energy. This is because a higher bond order means more electrons are being shared between the atoms, leading to greater electrostatic attraction and stronger bonding. Verdict: Incorrect.

The s-character of a bond refers to the degree to which s-orbitals are involved in the hybridization of orbitals forming the bond. Bonds with higher s-character are stronger because s-orbitals are closer to the nucleus and have better overlap, resulting in stronger bonding interactions. However, while s-character does influence bond strength, other factors also play significant roles. It's important to note that s-character is more relevant in describing covalent bonds, not lone pairs, which the original question was about.Incorrect.

Lone pairs are non-bonding electron pairs present on an atom. They can exert repulsive forces on the electrons involved in neighboring bonds, leading to bond weakening. The repulsion caused by lone pairs can distort the bond angles and disrupt the effective overlap of orbitals, ultimately lowering the bond energy. As a result, an increase in the number of lone pairs around a bond decreases the bond energy. Correct.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has greater bond energy?

options.explanation: Hydrogen gas consists of H2 molecules, where two hydrogen atoms are bonded together by a single covalent bond. The bond energy of the H-H bond is relatively low compared to the other options.

Nitrogen gas consists of N2 molecules, where two nitrogen atoms are bonded together by a triple covalent bond. Triple bonds are stronger than single bonds, so the bond energy of the N-N bond is higher than that of H-H.

questions.text: Which of the following has trigonal planar geometry?

options.explanation: Sulfur dioxide has a bent or V-shaped molecular geometry due to the presence of two bonding pairs and one lone pair of electrons on the sulfur atom. It does not have a trigonal planar geometry. Incorrect.
Carbon tetrachloride has a tetrahedral molecular geometry, with four chlorine atoms arranged symmetrically around the central carbon atom. It does not have a trigonal planar geometry. Incorrect.

Sulfur trioxide has a trigonal planar molecular geometry. It consists of three oxygen atoms bonded to a central sulfur atom, with no lone pairs on sulfur. This arrangement results in a trigonal planar geometry. Correct.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has the maximum number of dative bonds?

options.explanation: The molecule that has the maximum number of dative bonds is B3N3H6.A dative bond is a type of chemical bond that is formed when one atom donates a pair of electrons to another atom. In B3N3H6, each of the boron atoms donates a pair of electrons to one of the nitrogen atoms. This gives each nitrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

H2O2 has two dative bonds. The oxygen atom on the left side of the molecule donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom on the right side of the molecule. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons. The oxygen atom on the right side of the molecule also donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom on the left side of the molecule. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.
NH4- has one dative bond. The nitrogen atom donates a pair of electrons to the hydrogen atom. This gives the hydrogen atom a complete octet of electrons.

In Al2O6, each aluminum atom is bonded to three oxygen atoms. The aluminum atom has a valence of 3, and each oxygen atom has a valence of 2. To satisfy the octet rule, each aluminum atom shares one pair of electrons with each oxygen atom. This results in two dative bonds in each aluminum atom. However, there are no other dative bonds in the molecule. The oxygen atoms do not have any lone pairs of electrons that they can donate to the aluminum atoms.

questions.text: Solid carbon dioxide (dry ice) is used as a refrigerating agent because it readily changes directly from the solid into the vapor state at low temperature. What does this indicate the main intermolecular bonding / forces in CO2(s) to be?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Covalent bonding is a type of chemical bonding that involves the sharing of electrons between atoms. This sharing of electrons allows the atoms to complete their valence shells and become more stable.Hydrogen (H2): Hydrogen atoms have one valence electron each. When two hydrogen atoms come together, they share their valence electrons to form a covalent bond. This gives each hydrogen atom a full valence shell of two electrons.

Ionic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that involves the transfer of electrons between atoms. This transfer of electrons creates ions, which are atoms that have a positive or negative charge. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other, forming an ionic bond.Sodium chloride (NaCl): Sodium (Na) is a metal that has one valence electron. Chlorine (Cl) is a nonmetal that needs one electron to complete its valence shell. When sodium and chlorine react, the sodium atom transfers its valence electron to the chlorine atom. This creates a sodium ion (Na+) with a positive charge and a chlorine ion (Cl-) with a negative charge. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other, forming an ionic bond.

Hydrogen bonding is a special type of dipole-dipole force. It occurs between a hydrogen atom that is covalently bonded to a highly electronegative atom, such as oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine, and another atom that has a lone pair of electrons. The hydrogen atom is attracted to the lone pair of electrons, and this attraction creates a relatively strong bond between the two atoms. Hydrogen bonding is responsible for the high boiling points of water and ammonia.

The main intermolecular bonding forces in CO2(s) are London dispersion forces. London dispersion forces are the weakest type of intermolecular force. They occur between all molecules, but they are only significant when the molecules are large or have highly electronegative atoms. Carbon dioxide is a nonpolar molecule, so it does not have dipole-dipole forces or hydrogen bonding. However, the carbon dioxide molecules are polar because of the difference in electronegativity between carbon and oxygen. This difference in electronegativity creates a temporary dipole in each molecule, which can induce a dipole in neighboring molecules. The interaction between these induced dipoles is what creates London dispersion forces. The strength of London dispersion forces increases with the number of electrons in a molecule. Carbon dioxide has 12 valence electrons, which is relatively high for a nonpolar molecule. This means that the London dispersion forces in CO2(s) are relatively strong. The strong London dispersion forces in CO2(s) make it difficult for the molecules to move past each other. This is why solid carbon dioxide is a solid at room temperature. However, at low temperatures, the thermal energy of the molecules is not enough to overcome the London dispersion forces. As a result, the molecules can escape from the solid state and enter the vapor state.
