questions.text: Which o f the following theories was put forward by Drude and extended by Loren (1923) to explain the properties of metallic solid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electron gas theory was put forward by Drude and extended by Loren (1923) to explain the properties of metallic solid. The electron gas theory assumes that the valence electrons of a metal are free to move throughout the metal, like a gas. This explains the high electrical conductivity of metals. The theory also explains the high thermal conductivity of metals, as the free electrons can carry heat energy throughout the meta.

Valence bond theory (VBT) is a model in quantum chemistry that describes the bonding in molecules in terms of overlapping atomic orbitals. The theory assumes that the electrons in a molecule are localized between pairs of atoms, and that the strength of the bond between two atoms depends on the overlap of their orbitals.

Band theory is a model that explains the electrical and thermal conductivity of solids. The theory assumes that the valence electrons of a solid are delocalized, meaning that they are not confined to any particular atom. Instead, they are free to move throughout the solid. The valence electrons of a solid can be thought of as forming a sea of electrons. The energy levels of the electrons in this sea are grouped together into bands. The bands are separated by gaps in energy.

Crystal field theory is a model that describes the splitting of the d orbitals in transition metal complexes. The theory assumes that the metal is surrounded by a set of ligands, which are negatively charged ions or molecules that interact with the metal through electrostatic forces.

questions.text: According to VSEPR theory:

options.text: The shape of molecules depend upon bonded electrons pairs
options.explanation: The VSEPR theory is a model that describes the shape of molecules based on the repulsion between electron pairs. The theory assumes that the valence electrons of a molecule are arranged in space in a way that minimizes the repulsion between them.

options.text: Pair of electrons attract each other in valence shell
options.explanation: Yes, pair of electrons attract each other in valence shell, according to the valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory. The VSEPR theory is a model that explains the shapes of molecules in terms of the repulsion between electron pairs in the valence shell of the central atom. The theory assumes that electron pairs repel each other, and that the shape of a molecule minimizes the repulsion between the electron pairs.

options.text: Pair of electrons tend to occupy such positions that minimize repulsion
options.explanation: The VSEPR theory assumes that each atom in a molecule will achieve a geometry that minimizes the repulsion between electrons in the valence shell of that atom. Let's try to understand with example PF5. Repulsion between the five pairs of valence electrons on the phosphorus atom in PF5 ​ can be minimized by distributing these electrons toward the corners of a trigonal bipyramid. Three of the positions in a trigonal bipyramid are labeled equatorial because they lie along the equator of the molecule. The other two are axial because they lie along an axis perpendicular to the equatorial plane. The angle between the three equatorial positions is 120 ∘ while the angle between an axial and an equatorial position is 90 °.
All above explanations.

questions.text: Magnesium oxide is used to line industrial furnaces because it has a very high melting point. Which type of bond needs to be broken for magnesium oxide to melt?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Coordinate bonds are formed when one atom has a lone pair of electrons and another atom has a vacant orbital. The atom with the lone pair of electrons donates the electrons to the vacant orbital of the other atom, forming a covalent bond.

The type of bond that needs to be broken for magnesium oxide to melt is an ionic bond. Magnesium oxide is an ionic compound, which means that it is made up of positive and negative ions that are held together by electrostatic forces. The magnesium ion (Mg2+) is positively charged, and the oxygen ion (O2-) is negatively charged. The two ions are attracted to each other by their opposite charges. In order for magnesium oxide to melt, the ionic bonds between the magnesium ions and the oxygen ions need to be broken. This can be done by adding heat energy. When the heat energy is added, the ions are able to move around more freely, and the solid magnesium oxide melts.

No, the type of bond that needs to be broken for magnesium oxide to melt is not a covalent bond. Covalent bonds are formed by the sharing of electrons between atoms. In magnesium oxide, the magnesium atom donates two electrons to the oxygen atom, forming an ionic bond.

Metallic bonds are formed by the attraction between positively charged metal ions and delocalized electrons. The strength of a metallic bond depends on the number of valence electrons that the metal atoms have. Metallic bonds are very strong. Metallic compounds are typically solids at room temperature. Here are some examples of metallic compounds: Iron (Fe) Copper (Cu) Silver (Ag).

questions.text: All of the following are correct statements regarding difference between polar and non-polar bonds EXCEPT:

options.text: (1)A non polar covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which the two atoms share electrons unequally (2)In a polar covalent bond, the two atoms do not have the same electronegativity. The electrons in the bond are therefore not shared equally between the two atoms, and there is partial positive or negative charge on either atom.
options.explanation: This is correct.A polar covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which the two atoms share electrons unequally. This is because the two atoms have different electronegativities, which is a measure of how strongly an atom attracts electrons. The atom with the higher electronegativity will pull the electrons in the bond towards itself more strongly, resulting in a partial negative charge on that atom. The atom with the lower electronegativity will have a partial positive charge.In a nonpolar covalent bond, the two atoms have the same electronegativity. The electrons in the bond are therefore shared equally between the two atoms, and there is no partial positive or negative charge on either atom.

The greater the difference in electronegativity, the stronger the polar covalent bond. Non-polar covalent bonds are formed between atoms that have the same electronegativity. The electrons are shared equally between the two atoms, so there is no dipole moment. Non-polar covalent bonds are typically weaker than polar covalent bonds.

options.text: (1). A polar covalent bond can have polarity.(2). A non polar covalent bond can have no polarity.

In this case, the electrons are shared equally between the two atoms, and there is no net dipole moment.

questions.text: All of the following are correct statements regarding difference between sigma bond and pi-bond EXCEPT:

options.text: Sigma bonds are stronger than pi bonds
options.explanation: Sigma bonds are stronger than pi bonds because the head-on overlap of orbitals results in a greater degree of overlap. This means that the electrons are more strongly attracted to both nuclei, which makes the bond stronger.

options.text: Pi bonds are weaker than sigma bonds
options.explanation: Pi bonds are weaker than sigma bonds because the side-by-side overlap of orbitals results in less overlap. This means that the electrons are not as strongly attracted to both nuclei, which makes the bond weaker.

options.text: in a carbon-carbon double bond, there is one sigma bond and one pi bond.
options.explanation: A carbon-carbon double bond has one sigma bond and one pi bond. The sigma bond is formed by the head-on overlap of two sp2 hybridized orbitals on the carbon atoms. The pi bond is formed by the side-by-side overlap of two p orbitals on the carbon atoms. The sp2 hybridized orbitals are oriented at 120 degrees to each other, which gives the carbon-carbon double bond a trigonal planar shape. The p orbitals are perpendicular to the plane of the sp2 orbitals, which gives the pi bond a linear shape.
All above explanations are correct.

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not also known as a dative bond. An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond formed between a metal and a nonmetal. The metal ion is positively charged, while the nonmetal ion is negatively charged. The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, which holds the molecule together.

This type of bond is often formed between a Lewis acid and a Lewis base. A Lewis acid is an atom or molecule that can accept a pair of electrons. A Lewis base is an atom or molecule that can donate a pair of electrons. In a coordinate covalent bond, the Lewis acid donates a pair of electrons to the Lewis base. The Lewis base accepts the pair of electrons, and a covalent bond is formed.

questions.text: All of the following statements are matched correctly for ionic and covalent bonds EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of elements with very different electronegativities while Covalent bonds are formed between atoms of elements with similar electronegativities
options.explanation: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of elements with very different electronegativities. The more electronegative atom will attract the electrons from the less electronegative atom, forming a positively charged ion (cation) and a negatively charged ion (anion). The attraction between the oppositely charged ions is what holds the ionic bond together. Covalent bonds are formed between atoms of elements with similar electronegativities. The atoms share electrons in order to complete their valence shells. The shared electrons are attracted to the nuclei of both atoms, which is what holds the covalent bond together.

options.text: Covalent compounds have low melting and boiling points and Ionic compounds have high melting and boiling points.
options.explanation: Ionic compounds have high melting and boiling points. This is because the strong ionic bonds between the ions require a lot of energy to break. Covalent compounds have low melting and boiling points. This is because the covalent bonds between the atoms are not very strong and do not require a lot of energy to break.

options.text: Ionic bond is directional while covalent bond is non directional.
options.explanation: Option C is incorrrect.Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of different elements with very different electronegativities. The more electronegative atom will attract the electrons from the less electronegative atom, forming a positively charged ion (cation) and a negatively charged ion (anion). The attraction between the oppositely charged ions is what holds the ionic bond together. Because ionic bonds are formed by the transfer of electrons, they are not directional. The ions are attracted to each other regardless of their orientation. Covalent bonds are formed between atoms of the same element or of elements with similar electronegativities. The atoms share electrons in order to complete their valence shells. The shared electrons are attracted to the nuclei of both atoms, which is what holds the covalent bond together. So this is directional.

options.text: Covalent compounds are poor conductors of electricity while Ionic compounds are good conductors of electricity
options.explanation: Covalent compounds are poor conductors of electricity. This is because the electrons are not free to move around. Ionic compounds are good conductors of electricity when they are molten or in solution. This is because the ions are free to move around and carry charge.

questions.explanation: Ionic bonds are formed between atoms of different elements with very different electronegativities. The more electronegative atom will attract the electrons from the less electronegative atom, forming a positively charged ion (cation) and a negatively charged ion (anion). The attraction between the oppositely charged ions is what holds the ionic bond together. Because ionic bonds are formed by the transfer of electrons, they are not directional. The ions are attracted to each other regardless of their orientation. Covalent bonds are formed between atoms of the same element or of elements with similar electronegativities. The atoms share electrons in order to complete their valence shells. The shared electrons are attracted to the nuclei of both atoms, which is what holds the covalent bond together. So this is directional.

options.text: When cations and anions approach each other, polarizability increases only
options.explanation: When cations and anions approach each other, polarizability increases only. Polarizability is the ability of an electron cloud to be distorted by an electric field. When cations and anions approach each other, the electron clouds of the two atoms are distorted by the electric field of the oppositely charged atom. This distortion allows for some sharing of electrons between the two atoms.

options.text: Sharing of electrons takes place to some extent only
options.explanation: Sharing of electrons takes place to some extent only. Even in the most ionic bonds, there is some degree of sharing of electrons between the cation and anion. This is because the electronegativity difference between the two atoms is never 100%. For example, the electronegativity difference between sodium and chlorine is 2.1, which is considered to be a very large difference. However, this still means that there is some electron density shared between the two atoms.

options.text: Highest ionic character is present in CsF only

It is calculated using the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms involved in the bond. The higher the difference in electronegativity, the more ionic the bond. CsF has the highest ionic character of any known compound, with an electronegativity difference of 3.2. However, even in CsF, there is still some degree of sharing of electrons between the two atoms.

questions.text: The compound which contains both ionic and covalent bond:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CH4 has covalent bonding. In covalent bonding, electrons are shared between two atoms. The carbon atom in CH4 has four valence electrons, which are arranged in four sp3 hybrid orbitals. Each sp3 hybrid orbital overlaps with a 1s orbital from a hydrogen atom to form a sigma (σ) bond. The four sigma bonds are arranged in a tetrahedral geometry around the carbon atom. The covalent bonds in CH4 are strong and stable, which is why methane is a gas at room temperature. The tetrahedral geometry of the methane molecule gives it a nonpolar shape, which means that the molecule does not have a net dipole moment.

The compound that contains both ionic and covalent bonds is KCN. In KCN, the potassium (K) atom is a metal and the nitrogen (N) atom is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference between potassium and nitrogen is 2.1, which is considered to be a large difference. This means that the bond between potassium and nitrogen is mostly ionic. However, the nitrogen atom in KCN also has a lone pair of electrons. This lone pair of electrons can be donated to the potassium atom to form a coordinate covalent bond. A coordinate covalent bond is a covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons.

H2 has a covalent bond. A covalent bond is a chemical bond that is formed when two atoms share electrons. In the case of H2, two hydrogen atoms share their valence electrons to form a single covalent bond. The two hydrogen atoms are then held together by the attraction of their nuclei to the shared electrons.

KCl has ionic bonding. Ionic bonding is a type of chemical bonding that involves the transfer of electrons from one atom to another. In KCl, the potassium atom (K) has one valence electron, which it donates to the chlorine atom (Cl). The potassium atom then becomes a positively charged ion (K+), and the chlorine atom becomes a negatively charged ion (Cl-). The oppositely charged ions are attracted to each other by electrostatic forces, forming an ionic bond.

A coordinate covalent bond is a covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons.

options.text: The first I.E. of Al is less than the first I.E. of Mg
options.explanation: That is correct. The first ionization energy (IE) of aluminum is 577.5 kJ/mol, while the first IE of magnesium is 737.7 kJ/mol. This means that it requires less energy to remove an electron from aluminum than it does from magnesium.There are a few reasons for this. First, aluminum has a larger atomic radius than magnesium. This means that the valence electrons in aluminum are further away from the nucleus and are therefore less attracted to it. Second, aluminum has a lower electronegativity than magnesium. This means that the valence electrons in aluminum are less strongly attracted to the nucleus than the valence electrons in magnesium. The lower first IE of aluminum makes it more reactive than magnesium. This is because aluminum is more easily able to lose an electron and form a positive ion. Magnesium is less reactive because it is more difficult to remove an electron from its valence shell.

options.text: The second I.E. of Mg is greater than the second I.E. of Na
options.explanation: Correct is the second ionization energy (IE) of sodium less than the second IE of magnesium.The first ionization energy is the energy required to remove the most loosely bound electron from an atom in its ground state. The second ionization energy is the energy required to remove a second electron from an atom that has already lost one electron. In the case of sodium and magnesium, the first ionization energy is lower for sodium than for magnesium. This is because sodium has a larger atomic radius than magnesium. The valence electrons in sodium are therefore further away from the nucleus and are less attracted to it.However, the second ionization energy is lower for sodium than for magnesium. This is because after sodium loses one electron, it has a filled 2p subshell. The valence electrons in magnesium are still in the 3s subshell, which is not filled. The magnesium atom is therefore more stable with two electrons in the 3s subshell than with only one electron. The higher second ionization energy of sodium makes it less reactive than magnesium. This is because sodium is less easily able to lose a second electron and form a doubly positive ion. Magnesium is more reactive because it is more easily able to lose two electrons and form a doubly positive ion.

The transfer of an electron from an atom with a low ionization energy (IE) to an atom with a high electron affinity (EA) is called an ionic bond. An atom with a low ionization energy is easily able to lose an electron. This is because the valence electrons in the atom are not held very tightly to the nucleus. An atom with a high electron affinity is easily able to gain an electron. This is because the atom has a strong attraction for electrons. When an atom with a low ionization energy loses an electron, it becomes a positively charged ion. This ion is called a cation. When an atom with a high electron affinity gains an electron, it becomes a negatively charged ion. This ion is called an anion. The cation and anion are attracted to each other by the electrostatic force between their opposite charges. This force holds the ions together in an ionic bond. Ionic bonds are found in many common compounds, such as sodium chloride (NaCl), magnesium oxide (MgO), and calcium carbonate (CaCO3).

questions.text: The intermolecular distance is higher in:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The intermolecular distance is higher in gases. Intermolecular distance is the space between two molecules. In gases, the molecules are very far apart, with an average distance of about 100 pm. This is because the molecules in a gas have a lot of kinetic energy, which allows them to move around very quickly. The kinetic energy of the molecules is the energy they have due to their motion.

In solids, the molecules are much closer together, with an average distance of about 0.2-0.3 nm. This is because the molecules in a solid are held in place by strong intermolecular forces. Intermolecular forces are the forces of attraction between molecules.

Plasma is a state of matter that is not found naturally on Earth. It is a gas that has been heated to a very high temperature, such that the electrons have been stripped from the atoms. This leaves a gas of positively charged ions and free electrons. The intermolecular distance in plasma is very large, about 100 nm. This is because the ions and electrons are moving around very quickly and are not attracted to each other.

In liquids, the molecules are closer together than in gases, but not as close together as in solids. The average intermolecular distance in a liquid is about 0.3-0.5 nm. This is because the molecules in a liquid are able to move around more than the molecules in a solid, but not as much as the molecules in a gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The inter-titan distance, R, is the distance between the centers of two titanium atoms in a crystal lattice. It is determined by the size of the titanium atoms and the type of bonding between them. In titanium dioxide (TiO2), the inter-titan distance is typically equal to the sum of the radii of the two titanium atoms. This is because the titanium atoms are held together by strong covalent bonds. The stronger the covalent bond, the closer the titanium atoms will be together.

The sum of cationic and anionic radius in a crystal lattice is equal to the interionic distance. The interionic distance is the distance between the centers of two ions in a crystal lattice. It is determined by the size of the ions and the type of bonding between them.Ionic solids, the interionic distance is typically equal to the sum of the radii of the cation and anion. This is because the ions are held together by the electrostatic force between their opposite charges. The stronger the electrostatic force, the closer the ions will be together.In covalent solids, the interionic distance is typically smaller than the sum of the radii of the cation and anion. This is because the electrons are shared between the atoms, which reduces the effective size of the ions.

The inter-cationic distance, R+, is the distance between the centers of two cations in a crystal lattice. It is determined by the size of the cations and the type of bonding between them.In ionic solids, the inter-cationic distance is typically equal to the sum of the radii of the two cations. This is because the cations are held together by the electrostatic force between their positive charges. The stronger the electrostatic force, the closer the cations will be together.In covalent solids, the inter-cationic distance is typically smaller than the sum of the radii of the two cations. This is because the electrons are shared between the atoms, which reduces the effective size of the cations.

The inter-anionic distance, R-, is the distance between the centers of two anions in a crystal lattice. It is determined by the size of the anions and the type of bonding between them. In ionic solids, the inter-anionic distance is typically equal to the sum of the radii of the two anions. This is because the anions are held together by the electrostatic force between their negative charges. The stronger the electrostatic force, the closer the anions will be together.In covalent solids, the inter-anionic distance is typically smaller than the sum of the radii of the two anions. This is because the electrons are shared between the atoms, which reduces the effective size of the anions.

questions.text: The bond formed b/w NH3and BF3is co-ordinate covalent bond because NH3 is_ due to presence of one lone pair:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An acceptor species is a molecule or ion that can accept electrons. Acceptor species are typically electron-deficient, meaning they have a valence shell that is not completely filled with electrons. This makes them capable of accepting electrons from other molecules or ions. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

A donor species is a substance that can donate electrons to another substance. This type of bonding is called a coordinate covalent bond. The bond formed between ammonia (NH3) and boron trifluoride (BF3) is a coordinate covalent bond because NH3 is a donor due to the presence of one lone pair of electrons. A coordinate covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which one atom donates both of the shared electrons. In the case of NH3 and BF3, the nitrogen atom in NH3 donates its lone pair of electrons to the boron atom in BF3. The boron atom in BF3 has a vacant valence orbital, and it is able to accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is shown below: [N ::: H]--> F : B .The arrow indicates that the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom is being donated to the boron atom. The coordinate covalent bond between NH3 and BF3 is responsible for the formation of the compound ammonium trifluoride (NH4F). Ammonium trifluoride is a white solid that is soluble in water. It is used as a catalyst in the production of nitrogen-containing compounds. The nitrogen atom in NH3 is a donor because it has a lone pair of electrons that it can donate to another atom. The boron atom in BF3 is an acceptor because it has a vacant valence orbital that can accept the lone pair of electrons from the nitrogen atom.

.A neutral species is an atom or molecule that has a net charge of zero. This means that the number of electrons in the atom or molecule is equal to the number of protons in the nucleus. Neutral species are the most common type of atom or molecule in the universe. They are found in all three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas. Neutral species are also the building blocks of all more complex molecules.

A negatively charged particle is called an anion. An anion is formed when an atom gains one or more electrons. The extra electrons make the atom negatively charged. An atom can gain electrons by reacting with a more electropositive atom. Electropositive atoms are atoms that are more likely to lose electrons. For example, a chlorine atom can gain one electron from a sodium atom to form the chloride ion (Cl-).

It readily accepts a pair of electrons from other atoms, forming coordinate covalent bonds, to complete its octet and achieve a stable electron configuration. By accepting an electron pair from three fluorine atoms, boron achieves a full valence shell and forms three covalent bonds, resulting in the BF3 molecule.

questions.text: If the difference of electronegativity is 1.7 or more than that, the bond formed is said to be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct option.
Incorrect option.
Incorrect option.
Incorrect option.

questions.explanation: An ionic bond is formed when the electronegativity difference between two atoms is 1.7 or more. In an ionic bond, one atom (typically a metal) has a significantly lower electronegativity and tends to lose electrons to become a positively charged ion (cation), while the other atom (typically a nonmetal) has a higher electronegativity and tends to gain those electrons, becoming a negatively charged ion (anion). The strong electrostatic attraction between the oppositely charged ions results in the formation of ionic compounds with high melting and boiling points and typically solid at room temperature.

It focuses on electron-pair sharing, emphasizing the role of valence electrons in chemical bonding. According to this concept, elements tend to form bonds by sharing, donating, or accepting electrons to achieve stable electron configurations, typically with the goal of obtaining a full valence shell, which leads to the formation of covalent, ionic, or coordinate covalent bonds.

questions.text: Addition of second electron to a uni-negative ion always_?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct option.
Incorrect option.
Incorrect option.
Incorrect option.

questions.explanation: The addition of a second electron to a uninegative ion (an ion with a single negative charge, such as F⁻) is always an endothermic process. This means that it requires an input of energy. The reason for this is that adding a second electron to an ion with one negative charge results in increased electron-electron repulsions, which require energy to overcome. Additionally, the second electron enters a higher energy orbital, contributing to the endothermic nature of the process.

questions.text: The bond formed by the complete transfer of electron is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An ionic bond is formed by the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another. In this bond, one atom donates electrons to become a positively charged ion (cation), and the other atom accepts those electrons to become a negatively charged ion (anion). The resulting electrostatic attraction between the oppositely charged ions creates the ionic bond.

A covalent bond is formed when atoms share electrons between them. In this type of bond, the electrons are not fully transferred but rather shared between the atoms, resulting in a stable electron configuration for both atoms.

A metallic bond is the bond that holds metal atoms together in a metal lattice. In this bond, electrons are delocalized and move freely among the metal atoms, creating a "sea of electrons" that holds the metal ions together.

A polar covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which the electrons are shared unequally between the atoms. One atom has a stronger pull on the shared electrons, leading to partial charges (δ+ and δ-) on the atoms. The bond has a slight ionic character due to the unequal sharing of electrons. A polar covalent bond is a type of covalent bond in which the electrons are shared unequally between the atoms. One atom has a stronger pull on the shared electrons, leading to partial charges (δ+ and δ-) on the atoms. The bond has a slight ionic character due to the unequal sharing of electrons.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A) Ionic bond. An ionic bond is formed by the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of oppositely charged ions that are held together by electrostatic forces.

questions.text: Potential energy of a system comes from the:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Van der Waals forces are weak intermolecular forces that arise from temporary fluctuations in electron distribution within molecules. These forces are relatively weak compared to chemical bonds, and they play a role in determining the physical properties of substances, such as boiling points and melting points. However, van der Waals forces do not contribute significantly to the potential energy of a system.

Option B is the correct answer. The potential energy of a system primarily comes from the chemical bonds between molecules. Chemical bonds, such as covalent bonds and ionic bonds, involve the sharing or transfer of electrons between atoms, resulting in stable molecules or compounds. The energy associated with these chemical bonds is the potential energy of the system.

Ionic bonds are a type of chemical bond that occurs between ions with opposite charges, typically a metal cation and a non-metal anion. While ionic bonds contribute to the potential energy of a system, they are just one type of chemical bond, and the potential energy of a system comes from all types of chemical bonds, not just ionic bonds.

Option D suggests that the potential energy of a system comes from all three: van der Waals forces, bonds between molecules, and ionic bonds. While all these factors may contribute to the potential energy in different ways, the primary source of potential energy in a system is from the chemical bonds between molecules (as mentioned in option B).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B) Bonds between molecules. The potential energy of a system primarily comes from the chemical bonds between molecules. Chemical bonds involve the sharing or transfer of electrons between atoms, resulting in stable molecules or compounds.

questions.text: The unit of electron affinity is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A suggests that the unit of electron affinity is joules per mole (J mol^-1). Electron affinity refers to the energy change when one mole of gaseous atoms gains one mole of electrons to form one mole of gaseous anions. Therefore, the unit of electron affinity is indeed joules per mole (J mol^-1).

Option B suggests that the unit of electron affinity is kilojoules per mole (kJ mol^-1). This is the correct unit for electron affinity. It is common to express electron affinity in kilojoules per mole due to the relatively small energy changes involved.

Option C suggests that the unit of electron affinity is kilojoules per atom (kJ atom^-1). However, electron affinity is typically expressed per mole of atoms rather than per individual atom. Therefore, this unit is not commonly used for electron affinity.

Option D suggests that the unit of electron affinity is joules per atom (J atom^-1). Similar to option C, electron affinity is more commonly expressed per mole of atoms rather than per individual atom, so this unit is not commonly used for electron affinity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B) kJ mol^-1. The unit of electron affinity is kilojoules per mole (kJ mol^-1), which represents the energy change when one mole of gaseous atoms gains one mole of electrons to form one mole of gaseous anions.

questions.text: Energy is _when an electron is added to the isolated gaseous atom:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A suggests that energy is increased when an electron is added to the isolated gaseous atom. This statement is incorrect. When an electron is added to an isolated gaseous atom, the atom gains an electron, and the process involves the electrostatic attraction between the negatively charged electron and the positively charged protons in the atomic nucleus. As a result, energy is released.

Option B is the correct answer. When an electron is added to the isolated gaseous atom, the atom gains an electron, and this process involves the release of energy. This is because the electron and the atomic nucleus experience an attractive force due to their opposite charges, and energy is released as the electron is added to the atom.

Option C suggests that energy is decreased when an electron is added to the isolated gaseous atom. This statement is incorrect. As mentioned earlier, when an electron is added, energy is released, not decreased.

Option D suggests that energy is absorbed when an electron is added to the isolated gaseous atom. This statement is incorrect. The process of adding an electron involves the release of energy, not absorption.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B) Released. Energy is released when an electron is added to the isolated gaseous atom, as the electron and the atomic nucleus experience an attractive force due to their opposite charges.

questions.text: According to VBT, the essential condition for chemical bonding is that the orbitals of atoms participating in bond formation must:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A suggests that the orbitals of atoms participating in bond formation must be added. However, this statement is not related to the essential condition for chemical bonding according to Valence Bond Theory (VBT).

Option B suggests that the orbitals of atoms participating in bond formation must be multiplied. This statement is also not relevant to the essential condition for chemical bonding according to Valence Bond Theory.

Option C is the correct answer. According to Valence Bond Theory, the essential condition for chemical bonding is that the orbitals of atoms participating in bond formation must overlap. Overlapping of orbitals allows the electrons to be shared between the bonding atoms, leading to the formation of a chemical bond.

Option D suggests that the orbitals of atoms participating in bond formation must divide. However, this statement is not relevant to the essential condition for chemical bonding according to Valence Bond Theory.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C) Overlap. According to Valence Bond Theory, the essential condition for chemical bonding is that the orbitals of atoms participating in bond formation must overlap. This overlapping allows the electrons to be shared between the bonding atoms, leading to the formation of a chemical bond.
